Example 1:
In accordance with the Department of Homeland Security's Digital Services Strategy, all software engineering work performed under this contract shall follow Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) practices. The contractor shall conduct Program Increment (PI) Planning events every ten weeks, bringing together all Agile Release Train teams to align on objectives and identify dependencies. Each Agile team shall operate in two-week iterations with clearly defined iteration goals established during iteration planning ceremonies. The contractor shall designate Release Train Engineers to facilitate ART-level events and support teams in removing cross-team impediments. The System Demo, conducted at the end of each PI, shall be open to all Government stakeholders and demonstrate integrated working software. The contractor shall maintain and update Team and Program backlogs within the Government's approved Agile lifecycle management tooling. Inspect and Adapt workshops shall be conducted at the close of each PI to drive continuous improvement and quantify the improvement backlog.

Example 2:
The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services requires the contractor to apply Agile methodology across all workstreams supporting the modernization of legacy claims processing systems. The contractor shall organize work into user stories, epics, and themes within a hierarchical backlog structure and shall demonstrate traceability from individual user stories to program-level epics and strategic objectives. Sprints shall be conducted on a two-week cadence with sprint planning occurring on the first day of each sprint and sprint review and retrospective conducted on the final day. The Government's designated Product Owner shall have authority to accept or reject sprint deliverables based on the agreed Definition of Done. The contractor shall not carry incomplete user stories across sprint boundaries without documented justification and re-estimation. The contractor shall facilitate quarterly Agile maturity assessments using a Government-approved framework and shall present findings and improvement recommendations to the CMS program office. Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Continuous Integration practices shall be employed as integral components of the Agile engineering workflow.

Example 3:
The Department of Education, Office of the Chief Information Officer, requires the contractor to lead and execute a modernization initiative using Agile Scrum practices in alignment with the agency's Technology Modernization Playbook. The contractor shall staff an Agile delivery team that includes at minimum a Scrum Master, Product Manager, UX Researcher, Front-End and Back-End Developers, and a Quality Assurance Engineer. All work shall be organized into a continuously refined product backlog with epics, features, and user stories sized and prioritized by the Government Product Owner in collaboration with the contractor. Sprint cycles shall be two weeks in duration, and the contractor shall present a sprint demo to agency stakeholders at each sprint review, with attendance tracked and documented. The Scrum Master shall facilitate sprint retrospectives and shall maintain a retrospective action log accessible to the Government. Agile metrics shall be reported in the contractor's monthly status report, including planned versus completed story points, sprint goal achievement rate, and the number of impediments raised and resolved. The contractor shall provide Agile coaching support to Government personnel as a secondary deliverable to build internal agency capacity.

In support of the Department of Health and Human Services, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services modernization initiative, the contractor shall implement the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) as the governing methodology for all development activities conducted under this contract. The contractor shall organize all delivery personnel into Agile Release Trains (ARTs), with each ART comprising no fewer than fifty and no more than one hundred twenty-five individuals organized into multiple Scrum teams operating in synchronized two-week iteration cadences. Program Increment Planning events shall be conducted on a ten-week cycle and shall be held in person or via hybrid format at a CMS-approved facility. All ART teams shall participate in PI Planning and shall produce team PI objectives, iteration plans, and a program board identifying cross-team dependencies and risks for Government review. The contractor shall designate qualified Release Train Engineers to facilitate ART ceremonies, including System Demos, Inspect and Adapt workshops, and Scrum of Scrums. The System Demo, held at the conclusion of each Program Increment, shall present integrated, tested software to CMS stakeholders and shall serve as a formal checkpoint for Government acceptance of PI-level deliverables. The contractor shall maintain Program and Team backlogs in the CMS enterprise Agile lifecycle management tooling and shall ensure traceability from portfolio epics through features to individual user stories. Lean-Agile metrics including program predictability, feature cycle time, and iteration burnup data shall be compiled and presented during each Inspect and Adapt session to quantify delivery performance and identify systemic improvement opportunities.

IV.B – Technical Approach and Methodology Requirements. The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Environmental Information, intends to award a task order for the modernization of its legacy data management platform. In support of EPA's Enterprise IT Roadmap and digital transformation objectives, the contractor shall execute all development activities using Agile methodology, specifically the Scrum framework as described herein. The contractor shall not employ waterfall or other sequential development approaches for any software delivery work under this task order. The contractor's Agile teams shall operate within two-week sprints and shall deliver working, tested software increments at the conclusion of each sprint period. Sprint planning sessions shall be held at the start of each sprint and shall result in a documented sprint backlog with story-point estimates and a clearly articulated sprint goal agreed upon by the Scrum team and the Government Product Owner. Example 1:
NOTICE OF INTENT TO AWARD / REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS – DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, BUREAU OF PRISONS. The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Facilities Management Branch, solicits proposals for the repair and restoration of exterior masonry at one medium-security correctional facility in the Southeast Region. The scope includes inspection, joint repointing, surface cleaning, and application of waterproof sealant across approximately 4,000 square feet of affected surface area. This procurement will result in a single award firm-fixed-price contract. Available funding for this effort totals $88,000, which constitutes the full and complete contract ceiling. The BOP has determined that this project must be executed within that amount based on current capital improvement budget allocations for the facility. No modifications adding additional scope or funding will be entertained after award. Contractors must include a complete material and labor cost breakdown with their proposals and certify that the total proposed price, inclusive of all overhead, profit, bonding, and insurance, does not exceed eighty-eight thousand dollars ($88,000). Proposals above this ceiling will be rejected.

Example 2:
The Department of Justice, Federal Bureau of Prisons, Mid-Atlantic Regional Office, hereby solicits offers for the provision of mental health counseling and psychiatric evaluation services at a designated federal correctional institution in Cumberland, Maryland. The period of performance under this contract shall be nine (9) months from the date of award. There are no option years associated with this procurement. The contractor shall provide a minimum of two licensed psychiatrists and four licensed clinical social workers, each holding valid credentials in the State of Maryland or holding equivalent federal licensure. Services shall be provided on-site five days per week, with on-call availability for psychiatric emergencies. All contractor personnel must successfully complete Bureau of Prisons background investigations and security orientation prior to commencing work. Monthly performance reports are required and must be submitted to the COR no later than the fifth business day of each succeeding month. Award will be made to the responsible offeror whose proposal represents the best value.

Example 3:
SOLICITATION NUMBER: DOJ-BOP-FLW-25-0042. SET-ASIDE STATUS: 8(a) Business Development Program — Competitive. The Federal Bureau of Prisons, Federal Correctional Complex Forrest City, hereby solicits proposals for food service management and inmate dining support. This acquisition is conducted exclusively under the SBA 8(a) Business Development Program and is restricted to certified 8(a) participants in accordance with FAR 19.800 through 19.811. NAICS 722310 (Food Service Contractors) applies, size standard $47 million. The Government will not consider offers from firms that do not hold a valid, active 8(a) certification as issued by the U.S. Small Business Administration at the time of offer submission. Offerors must submit their current SBA 8(a) program certification documentation as part of Volume I of the proposal package. The base period of performance is twelve months with four twelve-month option periods. This solicitation will result in a firm-fixed-price contract. The agency has coordinated this requirement with the SBA as required under 13 CFR 124.501 prior to issuance of this solicitation.

Given the short duration of this requirement, the selected contractor must demonstrate the ability to assign qualified personnel to the facility within five (5) business days of award. Proposals must include a technical approach narrative, key personnel resumes, a staffing and scheduling plan, and a fully burdened price schedule. Evaluation factors include technical capability, relevant past performance, and price.

The Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Office of Facilities and Asset Management, requires comprehensive building operations and maintenance services for the CBP Land Port of Entry at Laredo, Texas. All contractor work shall be performed at the World Trade Bridge Port of Entry, 6002 Milo Rodriguez Jr. Blvd, Laredo, TX 78045. The physical and operational security demands of this mission-critical federal facility require that all personnel engaged under this contract be present at the port of entry during all hours of contract performance. No work may be conducted from off-site locations. Personnel must complete CBP-specific security training and receive a facility access badge before performing any duties. The contractor shall maintain a dedicated on-site supervisor during all operational shifts. Maintenance logs, inspection records, and service reports shall be completed on-site and submitted to the COR through the government's facility management system, which is accessible only from terminals located within the port of entry. Contractors found to be conducting contract-related work from unauthorized locations will be subject to immediate removal from the program.

Example 2:
The Department of Homeland Security anticipates award of a single Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract to support cybersecurity operations at multiple field offices across the continental United States. The successful offeror shall provide no fewer than twelve (12) full-time equivalent personnel, including a Program Manager, Deputy Program Manager, four (4) Senior Cybersecurity Analysts, three (3) Network Engineers, and at least two (2) Vulnerability Assessment Specialists. All personnel must maintain active Secret clearances at time of contract award. The contractor shall ensure continuous coverage during core business hours (0700–1800 local time) and shall provide on-call support during off-hours for critical incident response. The Government anticipates that the breadth of work, spanning threat monitoring, penetration testing, compliance reporting, and stakeholder coordination, will necessitate the full complement of staff described herein throughout the base period and all option years. Offerors whose proposed staffing plans reflect fewer than twelve personnel will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. The offeror shall submit a staffing plan as Volume III of the technical proposal, clearly identifying each labor category, the individual proposed, and their respective roles and responsibilities in support of this requirement.

Example 3:
DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY
U.S. CUSTOMS AND BORDER PROTECTION
SOLICITATION: 70B04C24R00000025

This requirement is set aside exclusively for Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) concerns in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 19.1405. U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) intends to procure professional training and curriculum development services to support officer training programs at the CBP Academy, Glynco, Georgia. Contractors responding to this solicitation must be registered in SAM.gov and must have a current SDVOSB self-certification on file. The applicable NAICS code is 611430 (Professional and Management Development Training) with a size standard of $12 million. The anticipated contract type is a time-and-materials contract with labor hour provisions. Offers from any firm that does not qualify as a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business will be considered nonresponsive and removed from consideration. The contracting officer has made the determination that this set-aside is consistent with DHS acquisition policy and that competition restricted to SDVOSB firms will yield offers at reasonable prices reflective of the current market.

San Ysidro Blvd, San Ysidro, California 92173. All contractor personnel assigned under this contract shall perform work exclusively at the San Ysidro Port of Entry facility. The Contracting Officer's Representative will be stationed at the San Ysidro facility and will conduct weekly performance reviews in person. Offerors shall submit a staffing plan that confirms the physical availability of all key and non-key personnel to report to the San Ysidro, California facility for the full base period and each option period. The Social Security Administration (SSA) announces a competitive procurement for modernization and operations support services for the Disability Case Processing System (DCPS). Contractors responding to this solicitation must understand that this effort constitutes continuing work on a system first placed into production in 2016. The existing codebase, comprising front-end Angular components and back-end services built on IBM WebSphere, will be transferred to the incoming contractor in its current state. SSA does not anticipate a full redesign during the base period; rather, the contractor is expected to maintain, enhance, and incrementally modernize the existing system in accordance with the Technology Modernization Plan attached as Exhibit C. The Government will facilitate a structured transition from incumbent contractor Leidos, providing access to all repository assets, architectural documentation, and operations runbooks. Proposals must include a staffing plan that accounts for immediate knowledge absorption from outgoing personnel during a 120-day transition period.

Example 2:
The Social Security Administration, Office of Systems, issues this Request for Proposal for the Disability Determination Services Workflow Automation Project. The contractor shall manage all development activities in accordance with SSA's Enterprise Systems Development Methodology, which prescribes a waterfall approach to system delivery. Project execution shall follow sequential phases as follows: Business Requirements Definition, Logical Design, Physical Design, Construction, System Integration Testing, User Acceptance Testing, and Production Deployment. Each phase produces mandatory deliverables that must receive formal written acceptance from the SSA Project Manager before work on the subsequent phase is authorized to begin. The contractor shall establish and maintain a fixed requirements baseline upon Government acceptance of the Business Requirements Specification. All proposed modifications to baselined requirements shall be submitted via the SSA Change Request process and are subject to impact assessment, Configuration Control Board review, and written approval prior to incorporation.

Example 3:
The Social Security Administration requires all IT development work performed under this Blanket Purchase Agreement to comply with SSA's Agile Development Standards. Offerors shall describe their Agile delivery model in the technical approach and demonstrate how their proposed methodology aligns with Scrum or Kanban frameworks. Contractors shall conduct sprint planning meetings with SSA's Product Owner to decompose epics into sprint-ready user stories with acceptance criteria no later than the iteration planning session preceding each sprint. The contractor shall update the Government's enterprise project management tool daily with sprint board status, impediments, and burndown data. Demonstration of working software to SSA stakeholders shall occur at the end of every sprint, and the Government reserves the right to redirect backlog priorities following each sprint review based on mission needs. The contractor shall implement Definition of Ready criteria to ensure user stories are sufficiently detailed prior to sprint commitment. Quarterly business reviews shall include Agile performance metrics, including average sprint velocity, defect density, and sprint goal completion percentage.

The selected contractor will be responsible for continuing work originally initiated under Blanket Purchase Agreement SSA-BEAT-14-0002 and must demonstrate the organizational capacity to absorb a large-scale government IT program mid-lifecycle. The existing system, deployed across 54 state Disability Determination Services offices, was built on a Java EE platform and Oracle database backend and currently processes approximately 2.8 million disability claims annually. SSA has conducted an independent technical assessment of the legacy codebase and will make this assessment available to all offerors under a Non-Disclosure Agreement prior to proposal submission. The Department of Transportation invites qualified vendors to participate in a mandatory design challenge as part of this solicitation process. Offerors shall develop and submit a working prototype of their proposed traffic management interface within thirty (30) calendar days of solicitation release. The prototype must demonstrate core functionality including real-time data ingestion, alert escalation workflows, and dashboard visualization. Government evaluators will conduct hands-on testing of each submitted prototype during a structured demonstration session scheduled at DOT headquarters in Washington, D.C. Scoring criteria for the design challenge component account for forty percent (40%) of the overall technical evaluation score. Prototypes that fail to meet minimum functionality thresholds as defined in Attachment C will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. Offerors are encouraged to review the Technical Evaluation Criteria in Section M prior to initiating prototype development. All prototype submissions must be delivered via secure government portal no later than 5:00 PM Eastern Time on the date specified in the solicitation timeline.

Example 2:
The Department of Transportation Federal Highway Administration hereby requests proposals for an enterprise asset management platform supporting the National Bridge Inspection Program. Consistent with FHWA's technology modernization policy, this acquisition mandates a COTS solution and explicitly prohibits any custom application development. Vendors shall not include development labor categories, software engineering hours, or coding deliverables within their proposed staffing plans. The work under this contract is scoped as configuration only, covering asset taxonomy definition, inspection workflow setup, geospatial layer integration using the platform's native mapping connectors, and report template customization through the product's built-in report designer. The Contractor must demonstrate that all proposed capabilities are available in the current production release of the software and do not depend on future product updates or vendor commitments. Proposals referencing software development lifecycles, code repositories, or testing environments associated with new feature creation will be considered outside the scope of this solicitation and may result in the proposal being found technically unacceptable.

Example 3:
The General Services Administration Office of Technology Policy is issuing this Request for Proposal for a cloud-based contract writing and acquisition management platform. This procurement is strictly limited to the acquisition of an existing commercial solution. No software development is authorized under any circumstances. Offerors shall not propose to build, code, or engineer new application modules in response to agency-specific requirements. The Government's intent is to leverage a proven, market-available product and adapt internal business processes to align with the tool's existing capabilities. Configuration of user permissions, workflow routing, document templates, and integration with existing financial systems via standard APIs is permissible and expected. Offerors must demonstrate that the proposed platform has been deployed in at least three federal agency environments. Proposals that include statements of work referencing sprint cycles, agile development backlogs, or custom feature engineering will be evaluated unfavorably and may be deemed non-compliant with this solicitation's scope restrictions.

The Department of Transportation invites qualified vendors to submit proposals for the development of a next-generation traffic management platform. This effort constitutes a full and open competition for a greenfield software development initiative. The Government has conducted extensive market research and determined that no existing system developed under prior contracts will serve as a foundation for this work. Contractors are explicitly not required to inherit, integrate with, or build upon any legacy codebase from predecessor programs. All architecture, data models, and application components shall be designed and built from inception by the selected vendor. The Government will provide relevant technical standards, interoperability requirements, and data exchange specifications that the new system must conform to, but these documents describe desired outcomes rather than pre-existing software artifacts. Prior vendors who supported the Department's older traffic coordination tools are eligible to compete but will receive no preferential treatment, and their historical familiarity with deprecated systems confers no contractual advantage. The selected contractor shall staff the program with personnel holding relevant domain expertise in transportation systems engineering and modern cloud-native development practices. The period of performance is anticipated to be 36 months with two optional extension years. Proposals should address the offeror's approach to agile delivery, DevSecOps pipeline construction, and user-centered design methodology. The Government anticipates a single award Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract with firm-fixed-price task orders. Questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing no later than 14 calendar days prior to the proposal due date.

SOLICITATION NUMBER: DOI-BIA-2024-0089

SET-ASIDE INFORMATION: This acquisition is a 100% HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside. Only offers from Historically Underutilized Business Zone (HUBZone) small business concerns are eligible for award. Offerors must be certified by the Small Business Administration as a qualified HUBZone small business concern at the time of offer submission and at the time of award. This requirement is being set aside for HUBZone small businesses pursuant to 13 C.F.R. Part 126 and FAR 19.1305. The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code for this procurement is 561730, with a corresponding small business size standard of $9.0 million. Contractors must maintain their HUBZone certification throughout the performance period. Proof of current SBA HUBZone certification must be included with the offer package. Awards made under this solicitation will count toward the agency's HUBZone contracting goals as established under the Small Business Act. Any offeror that is not a certified HUBZone small business concern will be considered non-responsive and will be rejected without further evaluation. Questions regarding HUBZone eligibility should be directed to the SBA at hubzone@sba.gov prior to submission.

Example 2:
DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY
Customs and Border Protection
Office of Acquisition
Solicitation Number: 70B04C24R00000021
Subject: Uniform Apparel Procurement

PROCUREMENT NOTICE — HUBZONE SET-ASIDE:

This requirement is designated as a HUBZone small business set-aside. Pursuant to 15 U.S.C. § 657a and implementing regulations at 13 C.F.R. Part 126, this solicitation is restricted to offerors that are certified HUBZone small business concerns. The Contracting Officer has conducted market research and concluded that the HUBZone set-aside is appropriate given the availability of qualified HUBZone firms in the uniform manufacturing industry. FAR clause 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole Source Award, is incorporated into this solicitation. The applicable NAICS code is 315990 with a small business size standard of 500 employees. Firms must hold a valid SBA HUBZone certification and ensure their SAM.gov registration reflects HUBZone eligibility before submitting an offer. Award will be made on a best-value basis. The estimated total value of this requirement is $3.1 million. Non-HUBZone offerors are not eligible to compete and will receive no further consideration upon identification as non-certified during the evaluation process.

Example 3:
DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS
Veterans Health Administration
Solicitation No. VA-258-24-R-0441

TYPE OF SET-ASIDE: HUBZone Set-Aside

Pursuant to the authority granted under FAR Subpart 19.13, this requirement has been designated as a HUBZone small business set-aside. The Government anticipates awarding a firm-fixed-price contract for medical transcription services to a concern that qualifies as a HUBZone small business. In order to be eligible for award, the offeror must be listed in the System for Award Management (SAM) as a certified HUBZone small business and must hold a valid HUBZone certification issued by the U.S. Small Business Administration at the time of submission. The contracting officer has determined that there is a reasonable expectation that at least two or more HUBZone small business concerns will submit offers at a fair and reasonable price. Accordingly, competition is being restricted exclusively to HUBZone-certified firms. Offerors are reminded that misrepresentation of HUBZone status is a violation of federal law and may result in debarment, fines, and criminal prosecution. The period of performance is twelve months with two option years.

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS, CONDITIONS, AND NOTICES TO OFFERORS

L.4 – JOINT VENTURE AND TEAMING ARRANGEMENTS

Offerors proposing as joint ventures must provide documentation demonstrating that the joint venture meets the applicable size and eligibility standards for this acquisition. This solicitation is set aside exclusively for HUBZone Small Business concerns pursuant to FAR 19.1305. Accordingly, each member of a proposed joint venture, or the joint venture entity itself, must be a certified HUBZone small business concern at the time of offer submission.

The contracting officer notes that several vendors inquired during the pre-solicitation phase whether the acquisition would be structured as a Women-Owned Small Business or Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business set-aside. After thorough market research, including analysis of vendors registered in the Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) database, the contracting officer determined that the HUBZone set-aside is the appropriate socioeconomic preference program for this requirement given the geographic concentration of the work and the demonstrated availability of qualified HUBZone firms. The EDWOSB set-aside authority was considered and formally rejected in the written acquisition plan approved by the Head of Contracting Activity. Firms holding only EDWOSB or WOSB certification without concurrent HUBZone certification are not eligible to compete under this solicitation.

RFP chunk to analyze:
SECTION B – APPLICABLE SMALL BUSINESS PROGRAMS AND ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS

This acquisition has been reviewed in accordance with FAR 19.203 and applicable Small Business Administration regulations. After conducting market research and reviewing the current procurement history, the Contracting Officer has determined that this requirement does not meet the threshold necessary to set aside the acquisition exclusively for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns. The market research conducted between January and March of the current fiscal year identified fewer than two WOSB firms capable of performing the full scope of work at a reasonable price, thereby failing to satisfy the two-offer rule required under FAR 19.1505. Accordingly, this solicitation is issued as a full and open competition. All responsible sources may submit offers. The Government encourages participation from all small business categories, including but not limited to Small Disadvantaged Businesses, HUBZone firms, Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses, and Women-Owned Small Businesses; however, no preference or set-aside status is being applied in the evaluation of this procurement. Offerors are not required to hold or maintain any specific small business certification to be eligible for award. Prime contractors awarded this contract are encouraged to provide maximum practicable subcontracting opportunities to Women-Owned Small Business firms consistent with FAR Subpart 19.7 and any applicable subcontracting plan requirements that may be negotiated post-award for contracts exceeding the subcontracting plan threshold.

Example 1:
Offerors in the competitive range shall be required to participate in an oral presentation as part of the evaluation process. The oral presentation will be conducted at the Department of Veterans Affairs Network Contracting Office and will be scheduled at the Government's discretion. The purpose of the oral presentation is to assess each offeror's understanding of the Performance Work Statement requirements, proposed staffing model, and quality control methodology. Presentations shall be a maximum of seventy-five (75) minutes in duration, which includes a mandatory fifteen (15) minute question-and-answer session conducted by the Technical Evaluation Panel. The proposed Contract Program Manager must attend and lead the presentation. Supporting staff may participate but shall not exceed four (4) individuals total. All slide materials or visual aids must be provided to the Contracting Officer no later than three (3) business days prior to the presentation. The Government may record oral presentations for evaluation documentation purposes, and offerors will be notified accordingly.

Example 2:
This solicitation for Healthcare Information Technology Modernization Support Services includes an oral presentation requirement as a formal element of the evaluation process. The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) will invite offerors in the competitive range to participate in structured oral presentations following the initial written proposal review. Oral presentations will be assessed under Technical Factor 2 – Implementation Methodology and Technical Factor 4 – Relevant Experience. Each offeror will have seventy-five (75) minutes to present, followed by a twenty-five (25) minute question-and-answer session conducted by the HHS Evaluation Committee. The offeror's proposed Program Manager, Lead Systems Architect, and at least one clinical informatics specialist must be present in person. Remote participation is not authorized except under emergency circumstances approved in advance by the Contracting Officer. Offerors should structure their oral presentation to directly address the evaluation criteria outlined in Section M and are advised that new information introduced during the oral presentation that was not contained in the written proposal will be evaluated as a clarification and not as a revision or amendment to the offer.

Example 3:
In accordance with the evaluation methodology described herein, the Department of Veterans Affairs requires each offeror to deliver a prototype submission of their proposed electronic health record integration module. The prototype submission shall demonstrate bidirectional data exchange between the offeror's platform and a VA-supplied test instance of the existing legacy system. Offerors must configure and host the prototype in a government-authorized cloud environment prior to the evaluation period. VA technical evaluators will conduct a structured review session lasting no more than three (3) hours, during which they will execute pre-defined integration test scripts and evaluate system responses. The prototype submission will be used to validate claims made in the written technical proposal; any discrepancies between the written proposal and demonstrated prototype functionality may result in a downward adjustment to the technical rating. Offerors must submit prototype access credentials to the Contracting Officer no fewer than five (5) business days before the scheduled evaluation session. Detailed instructions for the VA test environment configuration are provided in Section J, Attachment 7.

RFP chunk to analyze:
Section L.4 – Proposal Submission Requirements and Oral Presentation Instructions: The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) will require offerors determined to be within the competitive range to participate in an oral presentation as part of the overall evaluation process. The oral presentation is designed to assess the offeror's depth of understanding of VA healthcare IT modernization requirements and to evaluate the qualifications of proposed key personnel. Each offeror shall deliver a presentation not to exceed seventy-five (75) minutes, which shall include a structured question-and-answer segment of no less than twenty (20) minutes. The proposed Program Manager, Technical Lead, and at least one subject matter expert identified in the written proposal must be present in person at the VA Central Office, Washington, D.C. Requests for remote participation will be considered on a case-by-case basis and must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officer no later than ten (10) calendar days prior to the scheduled session. All slide decks, diagrams, and supporting materials to be used during the oral presentation must be delivered electronically to the Contracting Officer no later than forty-eight (48) hours before the presentation. Materials submitted after this deadline will not be considered. Evaluators will assess the oral presentation under the Technical Approach and Key Personnel evaluation factors, and scores derived from the oral presentation will be combined with written proposal scores to yield a composite technical rating. Offerors are advised that representations made during the oral presentation are considered part of the official proposal record and may be incorporated as contractual commitments upon award.

Example 1:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby issues this Request for Proposal for the modernization and ongoing sustainment of its legacy benefits processing systems. The scope of work encompasses system architecture redesign, application development, data migration, testing and quality assurance, training, and help desk support. Based on the complexity and mission-critical nature of this effort, the VA requires the contractor to provide a dedicated project team of no less than twenty (20) full-time personnel. The staffing complement shall include, at a minimum, a Program Manager, an Agile Release Train Engineer, five (5) software developers with demonstrated Java and cloud platform experience, three (3) database administrators, two (2) DevSecOps engineers, a cybersecurity SME, two (2) business analysts, a training lead, and requisite quality assurance personnel. All staff must be U.S. citizens and obtain a VA Suitability determination prior to commencing work. The offeror's staffing plan must demonstrate that all twenty positions will be filled within thirty (30) days of contract award, with acceptable résumés for each proposed individual submitted as part of the technical proposal. The Government reserves the right to reject proposed personnel who do not meet stated qualifications.

Example 2:
The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) is seeking a qualified contractor to configure and deploy a commercial off-the-shelf customer relationship management solution to support the Veterans Benefits Administration's case intake and tracking functions. The Government has determined that Salesforce Government Cloud meets the functional requirements identified in the market research phase and represents the most cost-effective approach. The contractor shall be responsible for configuring Salesforce workflows, custom objects, permission sets, and approval processes in alignment with VA business rules. No custom software development is anticipated under this effort. The contractor shall also be responsible for data migration from legacy Access databases, user acceptance testing coordination, and end-user training for approximately 400 VBA personnel across regional offices. The period of performance is a twelve-month base period with two twelve-month option periods. All work shall comply with VA Handbook 6500, FISMA requirements, and FedRAMP authorization standards applicable to the selected commercial solution.

Example 3:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby solicits proposals for the development and deployment of a patient scheduling optimization tool for use at one regional VA medical center. The total ceiling for this contract shall not exceed $85,000 for a base period of performance of twelve (12) months. No option periods are anticipated at this time. Offerors are advised that all technical deliverables, including requirements documentation, system design, development, testing, training, and post-deployment support, must be accomplished within the stated ceiling amount. The Government does not intend to award a contract exceeding this threshold under any circumstances. This acquisition is set aside exclusively for small businesses under NAICS code 541511. Interested parties should note that the contracting officer has determined that $85,000 represents a fair and reasonable price for the described scope of work based on independent government cost estimates. Proposals that exceed this maximum budget will be deemed non-responsive and will not be evaluated further. Offerors must clearly demonstrate how all required tasks can be completed within the $85,000 total contract value.

RFP chunk to analyze:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby solicits proposals for the design, development, and deployment of a custom case management platform to support the Caregiver Support Program (CSP). The Government requires development of a web-based system that integrates with existing VA enterprise services, including the Master Veteran Index and the Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA), to streamline caregiver enrollment, benefits tracking, and clinical coordination workflows. The contractor shall deliver a fully operational system capable of managing an estimated 500,000 caregiver records with role-based access controls, audit logging, and Section 508 compliance. The period of performance consists of a 12-month base period with two 12-month option periods. The contract type is a cost-plus-fixed-fee (CPFF) task order issued under an existing Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) vehicle. Offerors must demonstrate prior experience developing healthcare case management systems within the federal environment, including familiarity with HL7 FHIR standards and FedRAMP authorization requirements. The Government anticipates awarding a single task order to the offeror whose proposal represents the best value, considering technical approach, past performance, and price. Proposals shall include a detailed system architecture narrative, a staffing plan identifying key personnel and their qualifications, and a data migration strategy for transitioning records from the legacy system. All development artifacts, source code, and documentation shall be delivered as Government Purpose Rights in accordance with FAR 52.227-14.

Example 1:
The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) District Office in Atlanta, Georgia is issuing this Request for Proposals for web presence modernization services. The SBA seeks a contractor to deploy and configure a pre-built platform solution suitable for a public-facing informational website supporting small business resource partners in the Southeast region. The Government has conducted market research and identified WordPress as a viable commercial off-the-shelf solution that satisfies the accessibility, content management, and multilingual support requirements outlined herein. The contractor shall configure and deploy a WordPress-based site consistent with current Section 508 standards and OMB M-23-22 digital experience guidance. Scope of work includes theme configuration, plugin selection and security hardening, content population from existing materials, Google Analytics integration, and a two-year maintenance support arrangement. Custom software development is expressly out of scope. The contractor shall deliver a fully operational site within 90 days of contract award.

Example 2:
The Department of the Interior, Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA), issues this solicitation for website design and content management services for the BIA Office of Trust Services public information portal. The Government intends to deploy a commercial off-the-shelf content management platform, specifically Wix Enterprise or an equivalent commercially available solution, to support public-facing informational content delivery without incurring custom software development costs. The contractor shall configure and deploy the selected commercial platform, develop site templates consistent with USA.gov design standards and BIA branding guidelines, populate initial content provided by the BIA communications team, and establish a content governance workflow for ongoing updates. Proposals must address how the proposed pre-built platform solution achieves compliance with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act and WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards. The contractor shall provide a minimum of eight hours of training for designated BIA content administrators and shall remain available for platform administration support during the 12-month base period and two option years.

Example 3:
SOLICITATION: USDA-AMS-2024-MKTG-007. The United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Marketing Service, requests proposals for the development of a stakeholder outreach and communications plan to support the implementation of updated organic certification standards. The award will be a firm-fixed-price contract with a total contract value of fifty-five thousand dollars ($55,000), inclusive of all deliverables across the eight-month period of performance. Funding for this effort was appropriated under the AMS Market Development Program and has been identified and confirmed in that amount. The agency conducted an independent government estimate concluding that $55,000 is appropriate for the scope described in this solicitation. Offerors are put on notice that the Government cannot and will not augment this funding under any circumstances, including through supplemental agreements or modifications. A detailed work breakdown structure and cost justification must accompany each proposal, and proposed costs must not exceed the fifty-five thousand dollar total. Proposals offering reduced scope in exchange for lower pricing are not acceptable.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) Food and Nutrition Service (FNS), Mountain Plains Regional Office, is issuing this combined synopsis/solicitation for website redesign and content management services in support of FNS regional outreach and program communications activities. The Government has conducted market research pursuant to FAR 10.001 and has determined that a pre-built platform solution will satisfy the accessibility, multilingual content delivery, and public-facing communication requirements identified herein. Specifically, the Government has identified WordPress as a commercially available, off-the-shelf content management system that meets the requirements set forth in OMB M-23-22 and applicable Section 508 standards. The contractor shall configure and deploy a WordPress-based public website inclusive of theme configuration consistent with USDA visual identity guidelines, accessibility remediation, Spanish-language content support, plugin hardening in accordance with agency cybersecurity requirements, and integration with FNS national web analytics infrastructure. Custom software development is expressly outside the scope of this effort. The contractor shall populate the new site with existing approved content provided by the Contracting Officer's Representative and shall deliver a content editor training session for designated FNS regional staff. Full site deployment shall be completed within 75 calendar days of contract award. The period of performance includes a one-year base period and one twelve-month option period for ongoing maintenance and hosting support.

Example 1:
DEPARTMENT OF THE INTERIOR
BUREAU OF LAND MANAGEMENT
SOLICITATION NUMBER: 140L0424Q0051
ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING SERVICES

This solicitation is issued as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 19.1505. The Bureau of Land Management, Colorado State Office, requires environmental air quality and water quality monitoring services for approximately 1.2 million acres of federally managed lands in the Four Corners region. Only offers from firms that qualify as Women-Owned Small Business concerns under the SBA WOSB Federal Contract Program, 13 CFR Part 127, will be evaluated for award. NAICS Code 541620 (Environmental Consulting Services) applies, with a size standard of $19.0 million. Offerors must demonstrate current, active WOSB certification in the SBA's certify.sba.gov system as of the date of proposal submission. The Government will verify certification status independently; self-certification alone is insufficient. A Firm-Fixed-Price contract with a base period of one year and up to four option years is contemplated. Subcontracting plans are not required for this acquisition. All site visits and sampling activities must comply with BLM environmental protocols as detailed in the Statement of Work attached herein as Attachment A.

Example 2:
DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE
AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH SERVICE
SOLICITATION NO. 12981424Q0033
LABORATORY SUPPLIES AND CONSUMABLES

SET-ASIDE TYPE: Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB)

The United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service, Western Regional Research Center, hereby announces this solicitation as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside pursuant to FAR Subpart 19.15. Only firms that are currently certified as Women-Owned Small Business concerns through the U.S. Small Business Administration shall be considered for award under this solicitation. The requirement encompasses the acquisition of laboratory consumables, reagents, personal protective equipment, and general scientific supplies on an indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity basis. NAICS Code 423490 (Other Professional and Commercial Equipment and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers) applies, with a corresponding size standard of 100 employees. The estimated minimum and maximum order quantities are $50,000 and $500,000, respectively, over the base period of performance. Option years may extend the contract up to five total years. Offerors shall provide evidence of active WOSB certification in the SAM.gov and certify.sba.gov systems as part of their quote package. Quotes that omit certification evidence or that are submitted by firms whose WOSB certification has lapsed will not be considered. See the attached Statement of Work and Attachment 1 for the full catalog of required items and specifications.

Example 3:
ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY
OFFICE OF WATER
SOLICITATION NUMBER: EP-W-24-017
WATER QUALITY ASSESSMENT AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE

This acquisition is conducted as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside under the authority of 15 U.S.C. 637(m) and FAR Subpart 19.15. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Water, has determined through a rigorous market analysis that this industry falls within the categories designated by the Small Business Administration as underrepresented by women-owned businesses, thereby meeting the threshold requirements for a WOSB set-aside designation. Only firms that hold valid, current certification as a Women-Owned Small Business through the SBA's WOSB Federal Contract Program are eligible to compete. NAICS Code 541380 (Testing Laboratories and Services) applies; the size standard is $19.0 million. The EPA requires technical support services to assist states and tribes in conducting Clean Water Act Section 305(b) water quality assessments and Section 303(d) impaired waters listings. Deliverables include field sampling protocols, water quality data analysis, geospatial mapping products, and written technical reports. The contract vehicle will be a Time-and-Materials Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity contract with a ceiling of $12 million over five years. Minimum order: $100,000. WOSB certification status will be verified through official SBA systems prior to any award, and the Contracting Officer will document this verification in the contract file.

DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE
FOREST SERVICE
AGRICULTURAL ACQUISITION REGULATION (AGAR) PROCUREMENT

SOLICITATION NO. AG-84PD-S-24-0041
TITLE: ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE MONITORING AND REPORTING SERVICES — PACIFIC NORTHWEST REGION

SET-ASIDE TYPE: Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Set-Aside
AUTHORITY: 15 U.S.C. 637(m); FAR Subpart 19.15; 13 CFR Part 127

The United States Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, Pacific Northwest Regional Office, is soliciting offers for Environmental Compliance Monitoring and Reporting Services in support of ongoing land management activities within the Willamette and Mount Hood National Forests. This procurement has been designated as a Women-Owned Small Business set-aside. The Contracting Officer has made a determination supported by market research that competition restricted to WOSB concerns is expected to yield offers from two or more certified firms and that award can be made at a fair and reasonable price. Accordingly, only certified WOSB firms are eligible to receive award under this solicitation.

NAICS Code: 541620 (Environmental Consulting Services); Small Business Size Standard: $19.0 million average annual receipts. The scope of work includes wetlands delineation, stormwater compliance assessments, biological opinion monitoring, National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) documentation support, and preparation of annual compliance reports for submission to regulatory agencies. The contract will be structured as a Time-and-Materials/Labor-Hour hybrid contract with a base period of one year and three one-year option periods.

Firms must hold a current and active WOSB certification through the SBA at the time of offer submission and must provide their Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) as registered in SAM.gov. WOSB certification must be maintained throughout the entire period of performance. The Forest Service reserves the right to verify WOSB certification status at any point during contract performance. Any lapse in certification may constitute grounds for contract termination for default. Refer to FAR 52.219-29 (Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program) for applicable terms and conditions.

GENERAL SERVICES ADMINISTRATION — PUBLIC BUILDINGS SERVICE — NATIONAL CAPITAL REGION. This solicitation is issued to procure comprehensive moving and relocation services for federal agency personnel and office equipment associated with a consolidation effort at multiple facilities in the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area. The period of performance shall be five (5) months from the date of award. This procurement does not include option periods, and the Government does not anticipate any need for extension given the defined scope of the relocation project. The contractor shall develop and submit a detailed move management plan within ten (10) days of award, coordinate all logistics with agency POCs, provide all necessary packing materials and specialized handling equipment, and complete all relocations in accordance with the approved schedule. All moves involving classified materials or sensitive equipment shall be conducted under escort and in strict compliance with applicable GSA and agency security protocols. Proposals must include a technical approach narrative, a staffing and resources plan, and a price schedule reflecting all anticipated labor, equipment, and materials costs.

Example 2:
The General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, announces this solicitation for facility operations and maintenance services for a portfolio of seventeen federally owned buildings located in the National Capital Region. The combined gross square footage of the portfolio exceeds 4.2 million square feet, encompassing office buildings, courthouses, and a federal laboratory campus. To ensure adequate coverage and rapid response to maintenance needs across this geographically dispersed portfolio, the contractor shall provide a minimum staffing complement of thirty (30) full-time personnel. Required positions include a Contract Manager, an Operations Manager, six (6) licensed journeyman electricians, four (4) HVAC mechanics holding EPA 608 Universal certification, three (3) plumbers, two (2) elevator mechanics, two (2) fire suppression system technicians, a locksmith, a roofing specialist, and sufficient general maintenance workers to support all preventive and corrective maintenance tasks. The contractor shall also provide administrative staff to support work order management and reporting. All personnel shall be physically located within the National Capital Region and available to respond to emergency calls within two (2) hours of notification. Staffing plans must demonstrate no gaps in coverage for any building within the portfolio.

Example 3:
U.S. SMALL BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION
OFFICE OF PROCUREMENT
SOLICITATION NO. SBAHQ-24-Q-0027
ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT SERVICES

SET-ASIDE: This acquisition is set aside 100% for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns as authorized under 15 U.S.C. 637(m) and implemented through FAR 19.15. The SBA has determined through market research that this requirement falls within an industry identified by the SBA as underrepresented or substantially underrepresented by women-owned businesses, thus satisfying the industry designation requirement for a WOSB set-aside. The applicable NAICS code is 561110 (Office Administrative Services) with a size standard of $12.0 million. The SBA's Washington, D.C. headquarters requires administrative and logistical support services including mail management, records management, supply management, and conference support. Proposals will be evaluated using a Best Value Tradeoff source selection procedure considering technical approach, past performance, and price. Offerors are reminded that misrepresentation of WOSB status may result in criminal penalties, suspension, and debarment from federal contracting. Certification must be verified at certify.sba.gov prior to submission of offers.

PART I – THE SCHEDULE, SECTION A: CONTRACT FORM AND SET-ASIDE STATUS. This contract action is a Total Small Business Set-Aside as determined by the Contracting Officer of the Department of Energy, National Nuclear Security Administration, Office of Acquisition and Project Management. The Government solicits offers from eligible small business concerns only for technical writing, document control, and configuration management support services associated with nuclear facility safety documentation programs at the Savannah River Site, Aiken, South Carolina. The NAICS code applicable to this procurement is 541420 (Industrial Design Services) with a size standard of $19.0 million. Pursuant to FAR 52.219-6 and the policies established under FAR Part 19, no offer submitted by a business concern that does not qualify as small under the applicable NAICS code and size standard will be evaluated or considered for award. Offerors are advised that size status will be evaluated as of the date of offer submission and will be re-verified at the time of award. An offeror that misrepresents its small business status may be referred to the SBA Office of Inspector General and may be subject to debarment, suspension, and civil or criminal penalties. Registration in SAM with an accurate and current small business certification is a mandatory prerequisite to offer submission and award. The Government has conducted market research in accordance with FAR 10.002 and has determined that at least two responsible small business concerns are available to compete for this requirement, thereby satisfying the conditions for a total small business set-aside under FAR 19.502-2(a). Questions concerning this set-aside determination must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officer no later than five (5) business days prior to the solicitation closing date.

The Department of Homeland Security anticipates award of a single Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract to support cybersecurity operations at multiple field offices across the continental United States. The successful offeror shall provide no fewer than twelve (12) full-time equivalent personnel, including a Program Manager, Deputy Program Manager, four (4) Senior Cybersecurity Analysts, three (3) Network Engineers, and at least two (2) Vulnerability Assessment Specialists. All personnel must maintain active Secret clearances at time of contract award. The contractor shall ensure continuous coverage during core business hours (0700–1800 local time) and shall provide on-call support during off-hours for critical incident response. The Government anticipates that the breadth of work, spanning threat monitoring, penetration testing, compliance reporting, and stakeholder coordination, will necessitate the full complement of staff described herein throughout the base period and all option years. Offerors whose proposed staffing plans reflect fewer than twelve personnel will be deemed technically unacceptable and removed from further consideration. The offeror shall submit a staffing plan as Volume III of the technical proposal, clearly identifying each labor category, the individual proposed, and their respective roles and responsibilities in support of this requirement.

5. TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS AND DELIVERY

5.3 Service Delivery Model

The Government anticipates awarding a single Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract for professional advisory services in support of the agency's strategic planning function. The nature of advisory and analytical work under this contract is well-suited to flexible delivery arrangements. Contractor personnel may deliver all services through remote means, including teleconference, secure file transfer, and collaborative cloud-based platforms approved under the agency's FedRAMP authorization framework. The concept of on-site presence is inapplicable to the majority of tasks anticipated under this vehicle. Where individual task orders require unique handling, such requirements will be specified at the task order level and evaluated independently. Offerors should not assume that on-site performance will be a standard feature of any task order issued hereunder. The base IDIQ contract imposes no geographic restriction on where contractor personnel reside or operate. Proposals that describe a workforce distributed across multiple states or operating entirely from contractor facilities will be evaluated equally alongside proposals describing locally concentrated teams. The evaluation criteria in Section M do not include proximity to Government facilities as a factor. The agency values quality of analytical output and responsiveness over physical co-location. Video conferencing infrastructure sufficient to support real-time collaboration with Government stakeholders is considered adequate substitution for in-person engagement across all anticipated task categories.

The primary work under this contract is configuring and deploying the selected commercial off-the-shelf platform to meet DHS-specific reporting, resource allocation, and milestone tracking requirements. The contractor shall configure dashboards, automated notifications, role-based access controls, and integration connectors to existing financial management systems. The platform shall consolidate environmental sampling data from disparate site-level systems, provide a unified data repository with version-controlled records, enable real-time visualization of contamination plume monitoring data via a geospatial dashboard, and support regulatory reporting workflows in formats acceptable to EPA and applicable state environmental agencies. The contractor shall perform all software engineering, database design, geospatial integration, and user interface development activities, and shall coordinate closely with site environmental staff and federal project managers throughout the development lifecycle. The contract vehicle will be a single-award Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract with Time and Materials task orders issued for specific development efforts. The base ordering period is two years with two additional one-year options. Minimum and maximum ordering thresholds will be specified in the contract. Offerors shall provide a detailed technical approach describing their proposed architecture, data management strategy, security controls consistent with FISMA requirements, and approach to stakeholder engagement. Past performance submissions shall address comparable work involving environmental data systems, geospatial platforms, or scientific data management solutions for federal or state government clients. The Government intends to award to a single contractor following a competitive evaluation consistent with FAR Part 15 procedures.

DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
OFFICE OF ACQUISITIONS
Solicitation Number: ED-ODS-24-C-0087

The U.S. Department of Education, Office of the Deputy Secretary, is issuing this Request for Proposals as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) set-aside pursuant to FAR 19.1505 and the SBA's regulations at 13 CFR Part 127. The Department requires program evaluation and performance measurement services to assess the effectiveness of Title I grant programs across selected local educational agencies. This acquisition falls under NAICS code 541611 – Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services, with a size standard of $24.5 million. To be eligible for this award, an offeror must qualify as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business concern and must hold a current, valid EDWOSB certification issued by the SBA or a third-party certifier recognized by the SBA. Mere self-certification as a WOSB or EDWOSB is no longer sufficient; offerors must provide a third-party or SBA-issued certification as required under the SBA's final rule effective October 15, 2020. Proposals received from firms that do not meet the EDWOSB eligibility criteria will be rejected without evaluation. The anticipated period of performance is three years from the date of award, inclusive of options.

Example 2:
SOLICITATION NUMBER: HHS-2024-EDWOSB-0042

This acquisition is a 100% set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns under the authority of 15 U.S.C. 637(m). The Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Acquisition Services, intends to award a firm-fixed-price contract for the provision of professional health information technology consulting services in support of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services modernization initiative. Only offerors who are certified Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business concerns at the time of offer submission and at the time of award are eligible to receive this contract. Offerors must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov) and must have a current EDWOSB certification through the SBA certification program or a third-party certifier approved by the Small Business Administration. The applicable NAICS code is 541512 with a small business size standard of $34 million in average annual receipts. Any offer received from a concern that does not qualify as an EDWOSB at the time of offer will be considered nonresponsive and will not be evaluated. Questions regarding EDWOSB eligibility requirements should be directed to the contracting officer prior to the closing date of this solicitation.

Example 3:
DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
OFFICE OF SPECIAL EDUCATION PROGRAMS
SOLICITATION NUMBER: ED-OSE-24-C-0078
EDUCATIONAL RESEARCH AND EVALUATION SERVICES

NOTICE TO OFFERORS: This procurement has been designated as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) Set-Aside in compliance with Federal Acquisition Regulation 19.15 and the SBA's Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program, codified at 13 CFR Part 127. Only certified WOSB entities are eligible to submit offers in response to this solicitation. The applicable NAICS code is 541720 (Research and Development in the Social Sciences and Humanities) with a size standard of $22.0 million. The U.S. Department of Education's Office of Special Education Programs requires research and program evaluation services to measure the effectiveness of Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) funded state programs. Specifically, the contractor will design evaluation frameworks, collect and analyze quantitative and qualitative data, and produce policy-relevant reports for dissemination to Congress and the public. This is a Cost-Plus-Fixed-Fee contract with an estimated total value of $8.5 million over five years. Offerors must provide documentation of current SBA WOSB certification with their technical proposal. The Government will not accept third-party certifications in lieu of SBA certification for purposes of this set-aside.

Example 1:
The National Aeronautics and Space Administration, Goddard Space Flight Center, is seeking a contractor to provide engineering, integration, and test support services for an upcoming Earth science mission. The nature of the work, which includes systems engineering, instrument calibration, software verification, environmental testing, launch support, and post-launch on-orbit operations, necessitates a large, multidisciplinary team. The contractor shall maintain a team of at minimum fourteen (14) engineers and scientists throughout the performance period, with additional personnel required during critical mission phases such as environmental test campaigns and launch operations. The core team shall include systems engineers, mechanical engineers, electrical engineers, software engineers, and integration and test specialists with demonstrated spaceflight heritage. The offeror shall provide a staffing matrix in the proposal that maps each of the fourteen required positions to an individual, identifies their role, qualifications, and availability by contract phase. The Government understands that mission phases will drive variable workload and reserves the right to request surge staffing above the fourteen-person baseline; however, the minimum staffing level shall not fall below fourteen individuals at any time during the base period of performance.

Example 2:
The General Services Administration (GSA) Federal Acquisition Service seeks a contractor to provide software development and maintenance support for the System for Award Management (SAM.gov). This effort requires the contractor to work directly within the existing system environment, which has been under continuous development since 2012. The incoming contractor will execute a transition from incumbent operations currently held by IBM Federal, taking full responsibility for all active development sprints, defect backlogs, and release management activities. The legacy codebase includes multiple subsystems developed across several prior contract periods, resulting in heterogeneous technology stacks that the contractor must be prepared to support concurrently. GSA anticipates that the first six months of the contract will be heavily weighted toward stabilization and documentation of the existing system before new feature development proceeds at full velocity. The Government's Quality Assurance Surveillance Plan includes specific metrics for transition risk during this stabilization phase.

Example 3:
The Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), requires professional services to support the Air Traffic Organization's En Route Automation Modernization (ERAM) program. This solicitation covers continuing work on operational software that is currently deployed across 20 Air Route Traffic Control Centers nationwide. Contractors must have the technical capacity to engage with a mature legacy codebase written predominantly in Ada and C++, with some components dating to original system development in the late 1990s. The FAA will facilitate a formal transition from the incumbent program support contractor; however, the incoming team must be prepared for gaps in documentation that are common in systems of this age and operational complexity. Offerors must demonstrate past performance on programs of comparable scale where they have successfully taken over an existing system with safety-critical operational requirements. All proposed personnel in key positions must hold current FAA suitability determinations or equivalent federal security clearances at time of award.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), Goddard Space Flight Center, issues this solicitation for software engineering services in support of the Earth Observing System Data and Information System (EOSDIS) Core System (ECS). The selected contractor will assume responsibility for an existing system architecture that has been under continuous development since 1994 and currently archives and distributes in excess of 10 petabytes of Earth science data annually. This effort constitutes a transition from incumbent contractor Raytheon Intelligence and Space and requires the incoming vendor to take full possession of a legacy codebase comprising millions of lines of C, Perl, and Java developed across multiple NASA centers. The Government has prepared a Transition In Plan that the awardee must execute in coordination with the outgoing contractor during a mandatory 180-day parallel operations phase. NASA acknowledges that portions of the existing system interface with instrument ground systems via protocols that predate current data standards, and the contractor must provide a technical approach that addresses modernization of these interfaces while preserving archival data integrity. Offerors must provide verifiable past performance references demonstrating successful onboarding into comparable large-scale, operationally sensitive legacy systems. The technical evaluation panel will assess the depth and credibility of each offeror's strategy for managing transition risk and sustaining data continuity to the international science community during the handover period.

AGENCY SET-ASIDE DETERMINATION:

After conducting market research in accordance with FAR Part 10, the Contracting Officer has determined that this requirement shall be set aside exclusively for HUBZone small business concerns. This HUBZone set-aside is established under the authority of FAR 19.1305 and the Small Business Act, 15 U.S.C. § 657a. To be eligible to receive award under this solicitation, offerors must (1) be certified as a HUBZone small business concern by the SBA, (2) be registered in SAM with an active HUBZone designation, and (3) continue to qualify as a HUBZone small business through the duration of contract performance. The NAICS code for this acquisition is 541490, with a size standard of $19.0 million. Offerors must include their SBA-issued HUBZone certification number in their proposal cover letter. The Government will not consider offers from firms that do not hold an active HUBZone certification. Estimated contract value is $2.4 million over a base year plus four option years.

National Energy Technology Laboratory
Solicitation Reference: DE-SOL-0010988
Scope: Mechanical Engineering and Technical Support Services

NOTICE OF HUBZone SET-ASIDE: In accordance with FAR Subpart 19.13 and the Small Business Act, 15 U.S.C. § 657a, this solicitation is being conducted as a competitive HUBZone small business set-aside. The Department of Energy has determined that this requirement is appropriate for the HUBZone program based on current market research indicating the availability of multiple qualified HUBZone small business concerns capable of fulfilling the requirement at fair and reasonable prices. To qualify for this set-aside, firms must be certified as HUBZone small business concerns by the U.S. Small Business Administration. The HUBZone certification must be active and in good standing at the time of proposal submission. NAICS code 541330 (Engineering Services) applies with a size standard of $25.5 million. The Government will independently verify HUBZone certification status through SBA records. FAR 52.219-3 is included in this solicitation. Offerors must also comply with the HUBZone performance of work requirement, ensuring that at least 50% of the contract's labor cost is performed by HUBZone employees. The contract is anticipated to be a cost-plus-fixed-fee type with a period of performance of five years.

SOLICITATION NUMBER: DOI-BIA-2024-0089

SET-ASIDE INFORMATION: This acquisition is a 100% HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside. Only offers from Historically Underutilized Business Zone (HUBZone) small business concerns are eligible for award. Offerors must be certified by the Small Business Administration as a qualified HUBZone small business concern at the time of offer submission and at the time of award. This requirement is being set aside for HUBZone small businesses pursuant to 13 C.F.R. Part 126 and FAR 19.1305. DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION
Federal Highway Administration
Solicitation Number: FHWA-2024-R-00317

SET-ASIDE DESIGNATION: Total HUBZone Small Business Set-Aside

This solicitation is issued as a 100% HUBZone small business set-aside pursuant to FAR Subpart 19.13 and 15 U.S.C. 657a. The Federal Highway Administration is seeking qualified contractors to provide highway infrastructure inspection and data collection services along designated federal-aid corridors. Only firms holding a valid HUBZone certification issued by the U.S. Small Business Administration are eligible to submit offers under this solicitation. Offerors must be actively certified in the SBA's HUBZone program at the time proposals are submitted and must maintain that certification through the date of award and throughout the period of performance.

The contracting officer has conducted market research and determined that there is a reasonable expectation of receiving offers from two or more HUBZone-certified small business concerns at fair and reasonable prices. Accordingly, competition for this requirement is restricted exclusively to HUBZone small business concerns in accordance with FAR 19.1305(b).

The applicable NAICS code is 541330 (Engineering Services), with a small business size standard of $25.5 million in average annual receipts. Offerors must ensure their SAM.gov registration reflects active HUBZone certification status. The Government reserves the right to verify HUBZone eligibility directly through the SBA's certification database prior to making an award determination. Failure to maintain certified status at any point during evaluation will render the offeror ineligible for award. The anticipated period of performance is a 12-month base year with four one-year option periods. Clause FAR 52.219-3, Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole Source Award, is incorporated by reference and applies to this solicitation in its entirety.
