Example 1:
The Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command, Southwest, hereby solicits offers for refuse collection, recycling, and solid waste management services at Naval Base San Diego. This procurement is conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection methodology. Technical evaluation criteria are defined as Acceptable or Unacceptable and include: compliance with all federal, state, and local environmental regulations; demonstration of required equipment and staffing levels; and proof of required licenses and permits. Price evaluation will consider the total evaluated price across the base period and all option years. Award shall be made to the responsible offeror whose proposal is technically acceptable and offers the lowest price. The Government is not seeking best value tradeoffs and will not award to a higher-priced technically acceptable offeror under any circumstances. Offerors must thoroughly address all technical criteria to avoid an Unacceptable rating, which would preclude award regardless of pricing submitted.

Example 2:
The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Acquisition Solutions, is issuing this Request for Proposals for technical support services related to water quality monitoring and sampling at Superfund sites in EPA Region 5. This acquisition will be evaluated using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Technical acceptability will be evaluated under three pass/fail criteria: Key Personnel Qualifications, Laboratory Accreditation Compliance, and Quality Assurance Plan Adequacy. Proposals must be rated Acceptable on all three criteria to be eligible for award. Technical proposals that exceed minimum requirements will receive no additional consideration. Following determination of technical acceptability, award will be made to the responsible offeror submitting the lowest evaluated total price. The Government has made a written determination that LPTA is the appropriate evaluation approach given the well-defined nature of the work and the availability of qualified contractors capable of performing at minimum standards. Questions regarding the LPTA evaluation criteria must be submitted by the deadline specified in Section L.

Example 3:
This solicitation is issued by the General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, for grounds maintenance and landscaping services at federal facilities in the National Capital Region. Source selection will be conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) methodology as prescribed in FAR 15.101-2. Evaluation factors include Technical Capability and Price. Technical Capability will be rated either Acceptable or Unacceptable based on the offeror's demonstrated ability to meet the requirements of the Statement of Work. Price will not be evaluated for technical merit. Award will be made to the offeror submitting the lowest price among all technically acceptable proposals. Proposals rated Unacceptable under the Technical Capability factor will be eliminated from consideration regardless of price. The Government reserves the right to conduct discussions if necessary but intends to make award without discussions. All offerors must be registered in SAM.gov at time of offer submission.

Sprints shall be conducted on a two-week cadence with sprint planning occurring on the first day of each sprint and sprint review and retrospective conducted on the final day. The Government's designated Product Owner shall have authority to accept or reject sprint deliverables based on the agreed Definition of Done. The contractor shall not carry incomplete user stories across sprint boundaries without documented justification and re-estimation. The contractor shall facilitate quarterly Agile maturity assessments using a Government-approved framework and shall present findings and improvement recommendations to the CMS program office. Development shall proceed through formally defined sequential phases: User Needs Analysis, System Requirements Specification, High-Level Design, Detailed Design, Module Implementation, Unit Testing, Integration Testing, System Verification, and Operational Acceptance. Each phase shall be executed to completion before the subsequent phase commences, with no overlapping or concurrent phase activities permitted without written Contracting Officer authorization. A formal phase gate review board, comprising Government technical leads and the Contracting Officer's Representative, shall convene at the conclusion of each phase to evaluate deliverable completeness and authorize progression. The fixed requirements baseline, established upon Government approval of the System Requirements Specification, shall be placed under formal configuration management control in accordance with DOE-STD-1189. All subsequent modifications to baselined requirements shall be submitted as Engineering Change Requests, evaluated by the Configuration Control Board, and incorporated only through an executed contract modification. The contractor shall submit a comprehensive V-Model Project Execution Plan within 21 calendar days of contract award, documenting phase entry and exit criteria, verification and validation mappings, milestone schedules, and responsible personnel. Progress reviews shall be conducted monthly, with variance reporting required for any schedule or cost deviation exceeding ten percent of the approved baseline.

Example 1:
In accordance with the Department of Homeland Security's Digital Services Strategy, all software engineering work performed under this contract shall follow Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) practices. The contractor shall conduct Program Increment (PI) Planning events every ten weeks, bringing together all Agile Release Train teams to align on objectives and identify dependencies. Each Agile team shall operate in two-week iterations with clearly defined iteration goals established during iteration planning ceremonies. The contractor shall designate Release Train Engineers to facilitate ART-level events and support teams in removing cross-team impediments. The System Demo, conducted at the end of each PI, shall be open to all Government stakeholders and demonstrate integrated working software. The contractor shall maintain and update Team and Program backlogs within the Government's approved Agile lifecycle management tooling. Inspect and Adapt workshops shall be conducted at the close of each PI to drive continuous improvement and quantify the improvement backlog.

Example 2:
Under this task order issued against the NASA Solutions for Enterprise-Wide Procurement contract, the contractor shall manage and execute all software development activities in accordance with Agile methodology. The contractor shall implement a Scrum-based framework and shall operate all development teams on a uniform two-week sprint cadence synchronized with the program's release train schedule. Sprint planning shall be completed within four hours for a two-week sprint, and the contractor shall ensure that sprint goals are clearly articulated and agreed upon by the Government Product Owner prior to sprint commencement. The contractor shall maintain a Definition of Done that includes code review completion, unit test coverage thresholds, security scanning, and Product Owner acceptance. Working software shall be deployed to a staging environment at the end of each sprint for Government validation. The contractor shall produce and maintain an Agile project charter documenting team norms, Agile ceremony schedules, tooling agreements, and escalation procedures. Release planning artifacts including feature roadmaps and program increment objectives shall be reviewed and approved by the NASA Contracting Officer's Representative on a quarterly basis.

Example 3:
Pursuant to the Department of Defense's software acquisition pathway as defined in DoDI 5000.87, the contractor shall deliver capabilities using Agile software development methodology with continuous integration and continuous delivery pipelines. The contractor shall organize personnel into Agile squads operating in two-week sprints, with each squad responsible for a defined set of product backlog items. All Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, daily standups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives, shall be conducted in person or via Government-approved collaboration tools. The contractor shall employ a certified Scrum Master for each squad who is accountable for coaching team members on Agile principles and ensuring adherence to Scrum practices. A product roadmap updated at minimum quarterly shall be maintained and shared with Government stakeholders to provide visibility into planned increments of capability. The use of Kanban boards or equivalent visual management tools is required to provide real-time transparency into work-in-progress, blocked items, and completed work. Sprint velocity shall be tracked and used to inform release planning and capacity projections.

Section C.4 – Development Methodology. In furtherance of the Department of Defense Chief Information Officer's software modernization initiatives, the contractor shall adopt and rigorously apply the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) for all software engineering and integration activities conducted under this contract. The contractor shall establish an Agile Release Train (ART) encompassing all development teams assigned to this effort, operating on a Program Increment (PI) cadence of ten weeks. Prior to the start of each PI, the contractor shall facilitate a PI Planning event spanning two days, during which Agile teams collaborate to define PI objectives, identify feature dependencies, and establish iteration plans for each of the five two-week iterations within the PI. The contractor shall designate a qualified Release Train Engineer (RTE) responsible for facilitating ART-level events, managing risks on the ART board, and coordinating with Government stakeholders on release readiness. Each Agile team shall operate within two-week iterations with defined iteration goals established during iteration planning and tracked via team-level Kanban or Scrum boards maintained in the Government's approved tooling environment. A System Demo shall be held at the conclusion of each PI to present integrated working software to all DoD stakeholders and obtain formal acceptance of PI-level deliverables. The contractor shall conduct Inspect and Adapt (I&A) workshops at PI boundaries to perform quantitative and qualitative analysis of ART performance and produce a prioritized improvement backlog. Economic prioritization of features shall leverage Weighted Shortest Job First (WSJF) methodology to ensure highest-value capabilities are delivered earliest in the program roadmap.

The scope of work encompasses system architecture redesign, application development, data migration, testing and quality assurance, training, and help desk support. Based on the complexity and mission-critical nature of this effort, the VA requires the contractor to provide a dedicated project team of no less than twenty (20) full-time personnel. The staffing complement shall include, at a minimum, a Program Manager, an Agile Release Train Engineer, five (5) software developers with demonstrated Java and cloud platform experience, three (3) database administrators, two (2) DevSecOps engineers, a cybersecurity SME, two (2) business analysts, a training lead, and requisite quality assurance personnel. The contractor shall maintain a team of at minimum fourteen (14) engineers and scientists throughout the performance period, with additional personnel required during critical mission phases such as environmental test campaigns and launch operations. The core team shall include systems engineers, mechanical engineers, electrical engineers, software engineers, and integration and test specialists with demonstrated spaceflight heritage. The offeror shall provide a staffing matrix in the proposal that maps each of the fourteen required positions to an individual, identifies their role, qualifications, and availability by contract phase. The Government understands that mission phases will drive variable workload and reserves the right to request surge staffing above the fourteen-person baseline; however, the minimum staffing level shall not fall below fourteen individuals at any time during the base period of performance.

The General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, announces this solicitation for facility operations and maintenance services for a portfolio of seventeen federally owned buildings located in the National Capital Region. The combined gross square footage of the portfolio exceeds 4.2 million square feet, encompassing office buildings, courthouses, and a federal laboratory campus. To ensure adequate coverage and rapid response to maintenance needs across this geographically dispersed portfolio, the contractor shall provide a minimum staffing complement of thirty (30) full-time personnel. Required positions include a Contract Manager, an Operations Manager, six (6) licensed journeyman electricians, four (4) HVAC mechanics holding EPA 608 Universal certification, three (3) plumbers, two (2) elevator mechanics, two (2) fire suppression system technicians, a locksmith, a roofing specialist, and sufficient general maintenance workers to support all preventive and corrective maintenance tasks. The contractor shall also provide administrative staff to support work order management and reporting. All personnel shall be physically located within the National Capital Region and available to respond to emergency calls within two (2) hours of notification. Staffing plans must demonstrate no gaps in coverage for any building within the portfolio.

The total ceiling for this contract shall not exceed $85,000 for a base period of performance of twelve (12) months. No option periods are anticipated at this time. Offerors are advised that all technical deliverables, including requirements documentation, system design, development, testing, training, and post-deployment support, must be accomplished within the stated ceiling amount. The Government does not intend to award a contract exceeding this threshold under any circumstances. This acquisition is set aside exclusively for small businesses under NAICS code 541511. Interested parties should note that the contracting officer has determined that $85,000 represents a fair and reasonable price for the described scope of work based on independent government cost estimates. Proposals that exceed this maximum budget will be deemed non-responsive and will not be evaluated further. Offerors must clearly demonstrate how all required tasks can be completed within the $85,000 total contract value.

This procurement is strictly limited to the acquisition of an existing commercial solution. No software development is authorized under any circumstances. The Social Security Administration requires all IT development work performed under this Blanket Purchase Agreement to comply with SSA's Agile Development Standards. Offerors shall describe their Agile delivery model in the technical approach and demonstrate how their proposed methodology aligns with Scrum or Kanban frameworks. Contractors shall conduct sprint planning meetings with SSA's Product Owner to decompose epics into sprint-ready user stories with acceptance criteria no later than the iteration planning session preceding each sprint. The contractor shall update the Government's enterprise project management tool daily with sprint board status, impediments, and burndown data. Demonstration of working software to SSA stakeholders shall occur at the end of every sprint, and the Government reserves the right to redirect backlog priorities following each sprint review based on mission needs. The contractor shall implement Definition of Ready criteria to ensure user stories are sufficiently detailed prior to sprint commitment. Quarterly business reviews shall include Agile performance metrics, including average sprint velocity, defect density, and sprint goal completion percentage.

Example 2:
The contractor shall implement Agile development methodologies throughout the period of performance. All software development activities shall be conducted using Scrum framework, with two-week sprint cycles. The contractor shall conduct daily stand-up meetings, sprint planning sessions, sprint reviews, and sprint retrospectives in accordance with Scrum ceremonies. A certified Scrum Master shall be designated and responsible for facilitating all Agile ceremonies and removing impediments to team progress. The Government Product Owner shall participate in sprint planning and backlog grooming sessions to prioritize user stories and acceptance criteria. The contractor shall maintain a product backlog in a Government-approved project management tool and provide sprint velocity metrics as part of monthly progress reporting. All deliverables shall be produced incrementally, with a potentially shippable product increment demonstrated at the conclusion of each sprint. The contractor shall adhere to the Agile Manifesto principles and demonstrate continuous improvement through retrospective action items documented and tracked across sprints.

Example 3:
Under this task order issued against the NASA Solutions for Enterprise-Wide Procurement contract, the contractor shall manage and execute all software development activities in accordance with Agile methodology. The contractor shall implement a Scrum-based framework and shall operate all development teams on a uniform two-week sprint cadence synchronized with the program's release train schedule. Sprint planning shall be completed within four hours for a two-week sprint, and the contractor shall ensure that sprint goals are clearly articulated and agreed upon by the Government Product Owner prior to sprint commencement. The contractor shall maintain a Definition of Done that includes code review completion, unit test coverage thresholds, security scanning, and Product Owner acceptance. Working software shall be deployed to a staging environment at the end of each sprint for Government validation. The contractor shall produce and maintain an Agile project charter documenting team norms, Agile ceremony schedules, tooling agreements, and escalation procedures. Release planning artifacts including feature roadmaps and program increment objectives shall be reviewed and approved by the NASA Contracting Officer's Representative on a quarterly basis.

This procurement covers reforestation, timber stand improvement, and invasive species removal services within designated National Forest lands in Oregon and Washington. The North American Industry Classification System code is 115310 (Support Activities for Forestry) with a size standard of $9 million. Consistent with the FAR requirements applicable to SDVOSB set-asides, offers will only be accepted from businesses that have certified their status as a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov). The contracting officer has determined there is a reasonable expectation of receiving offers from two or more responsible SDVOSB concerns capable of performing the work at competitive prices. Contract type is firm-fixed-price. Performance bonds and liability insurance in amounts specified in the solicitation are required from the successful offeror prior to contract execution.

Example 3:
The contractor shall provide all personnel necessary to perform the scope of work described herein. All work under this contract shall be performed onsite at the Department of Agriculture's regional administrative offices located at 8030 Excelsior Drive, Madison, Wisconsin 53717. Remote work arrangements will not be authorized under any circumstances. Contractor employees must report to the Madison, WI facility no later than 7:30 AM on each scheduled workday. The Government will provide workspace, network access terminals, and badged entry to the facility. Contractors must comply with all Federal building security protocols, including identity verification and visitor management procedures enforced at the Madison location. Travel reimbursement to and from the Madison facility on a daily basis is not an allowable cost under this contract. The offeror shall demonstrate in their technical approach a plan for staffing qualified personnel within commutable distance of Madison, Wisconsin, or provide documentation of relocation plans for proposed key personnel prior to contract award. All deliverables, including reports, data analyses, and briefing materials, shall be completed and submitted from the Madison, WI performance site.

The Forest GIS Coordinator, acting as COR, will be present at the Quincy facility and will provide daily task direction and performance oversight. Proposals must demonstrate that all key personnel identified in the offeror's staffing plan are able and willing to relocate to or commute to Quincy, California for the full period of performance, including all option years.

All services shall be delivered exclusively on-site at Federal Correctional Complex Tucson, 8901 S Wilmot Rd, Tucson, AZ 85756. Given the inherent security requirements of a federal correctional environment, no contractor healthcare provider may perform any patient care, recordkeeping, or clinical consultation activities from a location outside the secured perimeter of the institution. Providers must report to the Health Services Unit at the start of each scheduled shift and must remain within the facility compound for the duration of their tour of duty. Telemedicine services are specifically excluded from this contract scope. Credentialing, scheduling, and supervision of all contractor medical personnel shall be conducted by the Health Services Administrator located at the FCC Tucson campus. Contractors must demonstrate their ability to staff all required positions with individuals who can physically report to the Tucson facility for every scheduled shift. Failure to maintain required on-site staffing levels may result in deductions under the contract's quality assurance provisions.

Example 2:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Veterans Health Administration, hereby solicits qualified contractors to provide comprehensive medical transcription services for the VA Medical Center located in Phoenix, Arizona. The period of performance for this contract shall commence on the date of award and continue through a duration not to exceed nine (9) months. No option periods are contemplated under this acquisition. Contractors must demonstrate the capability to mobilize a fully trained transcription workforce within ten (10) calendar days of contract award. All work shall be performed in accordance with applicable VA Handbook guidelines and HIPAA privacy regulations. Invoices shall be submitted monthly via the VA Financial Services Center. The Government anticipates award of a single Firm-Fixed-Price contract. Interested parties shall submit capability statements no later than the date specified in the associated solicitation notice on SAM.gov. The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code applicable to this requirement is 561410, with a small business size standard of $16.5 million in average annual receipts.

The Department of Justice, Federal Bureau of Prisons, Mid-Atlantic Regional Office, hereby solicits offers for the provision of mental health counseling and psychiatric evaluation services at a designated federal correctional institution in Cumberland, Maryland. The period of performance under this contract shall be nine (9) months from the date of award. There are no option years associated with this procurement. The contractor shall provide a minimum of two licensed psychiatrists and four licensed clinical social workers, each holding valid credentials in the State of Maryland or holding equivalent federal licensure. Services shall be provided on-site five days per week, with on-call availability for psychiatric emergencies. All contractor personnel must successfully complete Bureau of Prisons background investigations and security orientation prior to commencing work. Monthly performance reports are required and must be submitted to the COR no later than the fifth business day of each succeeding month. Award will be made to the responsible offeror whose proposal represents the best value.

Example 1:
The Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command, Southwest, hereby solicits offers for refuse collection, recycling, and solid waste management services at Naval Base San Diego. This procurement is conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection methodology. Technical evaluation criteria are defined as Acceptable or Unacceptable and include: compliance with all federal, state, and local environmental regulations; demonstration of required equipment and staffing levels; and proof of required licenses and permits. Price evaluation will consider the total evaluated price across the base period and all option years. Award shall be made to the responsible offeror whose proposal is technically acceptable and offers the lowest price. The Government is not seeking best value tradeoffs and will not award to a higher-priced technically acceptable offeror under any circumstances. Offerors must thoroughly address all technical criteria to avoid an Unacceptable rating, which would preclude award regardless of pricing submitted.

Example 2:
This solicitation is issued by the General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, for grounds maintenance and landscaping services at federal facilities in the National Capital Region. Source selection will be conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) methodology as prescribed in FAR 15.101-2. Evaluation factors include Technical Capability and Price. Technical Capability will be rated either Acceptable or Unacceptable based on the offeror's demonstrated ability to meet the requirements of the Statement of Work. Price will not be evaluated for technical merit. Award will be made to the offeror submitting the lowest price among all technically acceptable proposals. Proposals rated Unacceptable under the Technical Capability factor will be eliminated from consideration regardless of price. The Government reserves the right to conduct discussions if necessary but intends to make award without discussions. All offerors must be registered in SAM.gov at time of offer submission.

Example 3:
The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Acquisition Solutions, is issuing this Request for Proposals for technical support services related to water quality monitoring and sampling at Superfund sites in EPA Region 5. This acquisition will be evaluated using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Technical acceptability will be evaluated under three pass/fail criteria: Key Personnel Qualifications, Laboratory Accreditation Compliance, and Quality Assurance Plan Adequacy. Proposals must be rated Acceptable on all three criteria to be eligible for award. Technical proposals that exceed minimum requirements will receive no additional consideration. Following determination of technical acceptability, award will be made to the responsible offeror submitting the lowest evaluated total price. The Government has made a written determination that LPTA is the appropriate evaluation approach given the well-defined nature of the work and the availability of qualified contractors capable of performing at minimum standards. Questions regarding the LPTA evaluation criteria must be submitted by the deadline specified in Section L.

The Department of the Army, Installation Management Command, is soliciting offers for refuse collection and disposal services at Fort Campbell, Kentucky. The source selection methodology for this acquisition is Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Offerors will be evaluated under two factors: Technical Acceptability and Price. Technical Acceptability will be assessed on a pass/fail basis against the minimum requirements set forth in the Performance Work Statement. An offer rated Unacceptable under the Technical Acceptability factor will receive no further consideration, regardless of the proposed price. Price will be evaluated solely for reasonableness in accordance with FAR 15.404-1. Award will be made to the responsible offeror with the lowest evaluated price whose proposal has been determined technically acceptable. The Government will not perform a best value tradeoff analysis and will not consider superior technical approaches or added capabilities when making the award decision. Offerors are cautioned against proposing services or capabilities that exceed the minimum stated requirements, as exceeding minimum thresholds will not improve an offeror's competitive standing under this LPTA process. The Government intends to award without discussions; however, the Contracting Officer reserves the right to conduct clarifications or discussions if deemed necessary. All offerors must hold a current registration in SAM.gov and provide evidence of relevant past performance upon request.

Solicitation Number: 12-25-B-0034

The contractor shall deliver a minimum of three functional prototype units, all associated technical documentation, test reports, and a lessons-learned summary. All deliverables must be submitted to the NASA Contracting Officer's Representative no later than the final month of performance. Milestone reviews are scheduled at months two, five, and seven. Due to the compressed schedule, offerors must demonstrate immediate availability of key personnel including a Systems Engineer and Hardware Integration Lead. Proposals shall be evaluated on technical capability, schedule feasibility, and price.

Example 2:
The Department of Defense, Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, hereby solicits proposals under this Broad Agency Announcement and requires all offerors selected for Phase II negotiations to complete a mandatory prototype submission. The prototype submission shall constitute a functional hardware or software artifact demonstrating feasibility of the proposed technical approach as described in Section 3.2 of this solicitation. DARPA program managers will evaluate each prototype submission during a dedicated review session, which may include independent laboratory testing, performance benchmarking, and direct technical questioning of the offeror team. The prototype submission evaluation will assess innovation, technical merit, and readiness level advancement relative to the baseline technology described in the solicitation background. Offerors must ship hardware prototypes to the designated government laboratory no fewer than ten (10) business days prior to the evaluation session. Software prototypes must be delivered via approved media and must execute without modification in the government-provided test environment. DARPA evaluators retain the right to attempt to replicate offeror-reported performance results using independent test procedures.

Example 3:
The General Services Administration hereby issues this solicitation which includes a required design exercise component for all competitive range offerors. Specifically, each offeror must submit a prototype submission demonstrating their proposed citizen-facing web portal solution. The prototype submission shall be a fully navigable, hosted demonstration environment accessible via government-provided credentials. The prototype must include functional user registration, document upload, and status tracking modules. GSA evaluators will engage directly with the prototype during a structured two-hour evaluation session and will simulate end-user workflows as described in Attachment D, User Scenario Matrix. Prototype submissions are due no later than fourteen (14) days prior to the scheduled oral presentations. Offerors are advised that the prototype submission will be evaluated independently from the written technical volume and carries a weight of thirty-five percent (35%) of the total technical score. Any prototype that is unavailable, incomplete, or non-functional during the evaluation session will receive a rating of Unacceptable for that factor.

Section C – Description of Services

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) seeks contractor support for data management and analytics services related to climate observation systems. In reviewing industry feedback received during the Request for Information (RFI) phase of this acquisition, several respondents suggested incorporating a competitive prototype submission phase into the source selection process. After careful consideration, the Contracting Officer has determined that such an approach is not warranted for this requirement. The scope of work is sufficiently well-defined to allow meaningful evaluation through written technical proposals alone, and the Government lacks the budget and timeline necessary to administer a multi-phase acquisition that includes prototype development activities. Offerors should therefore not include prototype hardware, software builds, or demonstration materials with their proposals, as such submissions will not be evaluated and will be returned without review. The contractor selected for award will be expected to develop and deliver production-quality data pipelines, dashboards, and analytical reports in accordance with the detailed specifications provided in the Performance Work Statement, beginning at contract award.

The United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service, Western Regional Research Center, hereby announces this solicitation as a Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) set-aside pursuant to FAR Subpart 19.15. Only firms that are currently certified as Women-Owned Small Business concerns through the U.S. Small Business Administration shall be considered for award under this solicitation. The requirement encompasses the acquisition of laboratory consumables, reagents, personal protective equipment, and general scientific supplies on an indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity basis. NAICS Code 423490 (Other Professional and Commercial Equipment and Supplies Merchant Wholesalers) applies, with a corresponding size standard of 100 employees. The estimated minimum and maximum order quantities are $50,000 and $500,000, respectively, over the base period of performance. Option years may extend the contract up to five total years. Offerors shall provide evidence of active WOSB certification in the SAM.gov and certify.sba.gov systems as part of their quote package. Quotes that omit certification evidence or that are submitted by firms whose WOSB certification has lapsed will not be considered. See the attached Statement of Work and Attachment 1 for the full catalog of required items and specifications.

ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY
OFFICE OF MISSION SUPPORT
Solicitation No.: EPA-MSD-24-00156-EDWOSB

PURSUANT TO THE WOMEN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESS FEDERAL CONTRACTING PROGRAM, this solicitation is designated as a 100 percent set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns. The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) intends to procure data analytics and environmental monitoring software development services in support of the Office of Water's National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) program. NAICS code 541511 – Custom Computer Programming Services applies to this requirement, with a small business size standard of $34 million in annual revenue. The EPA's contracting officer has made an affirmative determination that setting aside this requirement exclusively for EDWOSB concerns is appropriate based on thorough market research conducted in accordance with FAR 10.001. To participate, an offeror must be certified as an Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business by the SBA or an approved third-party certifier and must be registered and active in SAM.gov. Offerors must upload their EDWOSB certification documentation to the secure file-sharing portal identified in Section L of this solicitation no later than the proposal due date. Award will not be made to any offeror that cannot demonstrate a valid, current EDWOSB certification at the time of award.

The contractor shall provide program integrity, audit management, financial analysis, IT systems support, and stakeholder communications services to enable CMS to effectively oversee a portfolio of regional contractors administering Medicare Parts A and B. The anticipated scope of work, based on historical task order volume and complexity, requires the contractor to maintain a core team of no fewer than twenty-two (22) full-time personnel. Required positions include a Program Manager, two (2) Deputy Program Managers each responsible for distinct functional areas, five (5) Program Integrity Analysts, three (3) Financial Analysts with CPA credentials, four (4) IT Specialists supporting audit management systems, two (2) Communications Specialists, a Quality Assurance Manager, two (2) Data Analysts, and supporting administrative staff. The contractor shall provide monthly staffing reports to the Contracting Officer's Representative certifying that the twenty-two-person staffing minimum has been maintained and identifying any vacancies, planned departures, or substitutions. The Government reserves the right to assess deductions from invoices for any period in which the contractor fails to maintain the minimum staffing level.

Example 2:
The Department of Justice, Executive Office for Immigration Review, requires comprehensive information technology support services to sustain and enhance court management systems used by immigration judges across more than seventy (70) immigration courts nationwide. The contractor must deploy a standing team of not fewer than eighteen (18) IT professionals to execute the full scope of work, which includes help desk operations, application maintenance and development, network administration, data center management, cybersecurity monitoring, and business intelligence reporting. The required staffing configuration shall include a Program Manager with PMP certification, a Deputy Program Manager, a Help Desk Manager overseeing a staff of at least four (4) Tier I and Tier II support specialists, three (3) application developers, two (2) database administrators, a network engineer, a cybersecurity analyst, a data warehouse specialist, and a configuration management specialist. The contractor shall maintain this minimum staffing level throughout all base and option periods. Proposed personnel must successfully complete a DOJ background investigation prior to being granted access to DOJ networks and systems. The staffing plan, including résumés for all eighteen key and non-key personnel, shall be submitted with Volume II of the technical proposal.

Example 3:
The General Services Administration Office of Information Technology requires offerors to demonstrate proven experience delivering projects using Agile development practices. The period of performance shall be executed through iterative Agile sprints of two-week duration. The contractor shall implement all five Scrum events: Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective, and the Sprint itself, with Government personnel invited and encouraged to participate in Sprint Reviews and Retrospectives. The contractor's Agile Coach shall work with the Government Product Owner to build and maintain a prioritized product backlog, ensuring that the highest-value features are delivered earliest. Acceptance testing shall be performed within the sprint in which development occurs, consistent with Agile testing best practices and shift-left quality principles. The contractor shall track and report on Agile health metrics including team happiness index, sprint goal achievement rate, and escaped defect rate. All tools used to manage the Agile process, including the backlog and sprint boards, shall be accessible to designated Government personnel at all times.

The Department of Education, Office of the Chief Information Officer, is requesting proposals for desktop and laptop computer hardware refresh services under this competitive procurement. In accordance with FAR 15.101-2, this acquisition employs the lowest price technically acceptable (LPTA) evaluation method. Technical factors consist of Hardware Specifications Compliance and Delivery Capability, both evaluated on an Acceptable/Unacceptable basis. Offerors must certify that all proposed equipment meets or exceeds the minimum technical specifications listed in Attachment A. Proposals that do not meet all mandatory specifications will be rated Unacceptable and removed from consideration. Among technically acceptable proposals, the Government will select for award the offer representing the lowest total evaluated price. Offerors should not include options or features beyond those required, as additional capabilities will provide no competitive advantage under this LPTA process. All hardware must be TAA compliant and originate from approved sources.

Example 2:
This solicitation is issued by the General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, for grounds maintenance and landscaping services at federal facilities in the National Capital Region. Source selection will be conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) methodology as prescribed in FAR 15.101-2. Evaluation factors include Technical Capability and Price. Technical Capability will be rated either Acceptable or Unacceptable based on the offeror's demonstrated ability to meet the requirements of the Statement of Work. Price will not be evaluated for technical merit. Award will be made to the offeror submitting the lowest price among all technically acceptable proposals. Proposals rated Unacceptable under the Technical Capability factor will be eliminated from consideration regardless of price. The Government reserves the right to conduct discussions if necessary but intends to make award without discussions. All offerors must be registered in SAM.gov at time of offer submission.

Example 3:
The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Acquisition Solutions, is issuing this Request for Proposals for technical support services related to water quality monitoring and sampling at Superfund sites in EPA Region 5. This acquisition will be evaluated using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Technical acceptability will be evaluated under three pass/fail criteria: Key Personnel Qualifications, Laboratory Accreditation Compliance, and Quality Assurance Plan Adequacy. Proposals must be rated Acceptable on all three criteria to be eligible for award. Technical proposals that exceed minimum requirements will receive no additional consideration. Following determination of technical acceptability, award will be made to the responsible offeror submitting the lowest evaluated total price. The Government has made a written determination that LPTA is the appropriate evaluation approach given the well-defined nature of the work and the availability of qualified contractors capable of performing at minimum standards. Questions regarding the LPTA evaluation criteria must be submitted by the deadline specified in Section L.

The General Services Administration, Federal Acquisition Service, Technology Transformation Services, is issuing this solicitation for web hosting and content delivery network services in support of digital government initiatives. Following a market analysis and acquisition planning review, the Contracting Officer has determined that the LPTA, or Lowest Price Technically Acceptable, source selection methodology is appropriate for this acquisition. The requirement is sufficiently well-defined such that the Government does not anticipate meaningful distinctions in value among technically compliant proposals beyond the minimum requirements. This solicitation establishes two evaluation factors: Technical and Price. The Technical factor will be evaluated on an Acceptable or Unacceptable basis. To be deemed Acceptable, the offeror must demonstrate compliance with all mandatory service level requirements, including uptime guarantees of no less than 99.9 percent, FedRAMP authorization at the Moderate impact level, geographic redundancy across at minimum two availability zones, and IPv6 compatibility as specified in Section C. Any proposal failing to satisfy each mandatory technical requirement will be rated Unacceptable and excluded from award consideration without regard to proposed price. Once technical acceptability is established, award will be made to the offeror presenting the lowest total evaluated price for the base period and all option periods. In keeping with the lowest price technically acceptable methodology, the Government will not conduct comparative assessments of technical merit among acceptable proposals nor pay any premium for capabilities beyond the stated minimums.

The contractor shall provide program integrity, audit management, financial analysis, IT systems support, and stakeholder communications services to enable CMS to effectively oversee a portfolio of regional contractors administering Medicare Parts A and B. The anticipated scope of work, based on historical task order volume and complexity, requires the contractor to maintain a core team of no fewer than twenty-two (22) full-time personnel. Required positions include a Program Manager, two (2) Deputy Program Managers each responsible for distinct functional areas, five (5) Program Integrity Analysts, three (3) Financial Analysts with CPA credentials, four (4) IT Specialists supporting audit management systems, two (2) Communications Specialists, a Quality Assurance Manager, two (2) Data Analysts, and supporting administrative staff. The Government reserves the right to assess deductions from invoices for any period in which the contractor fails to maintain the minimum staffing level.

This Performance Work Statement requires the contractor to execute all software design, development, and testing activities using Agile Scrum methodology in strict compliance with the agency's DevSecOps and Agile Transformation Initiative. The contractor shall establish and maintain Agile teams with a recommended team size of five to nine members, each including a dedicated Scrum Master and supported by a Government-designated Product Owner. User stories shall be written with clearly defined acceptance criteria using the standard 'As a [user], I want [functionality], so that [benefit]' format and shall be estimated using Planning Poker or equivalent relative sizing techniques. The contractor shall operate on a consistent two-week sprint cadence and shall not alter sprint scope once the sprint has commenced without documented consent from the Contracting Officer's Representative. Sprint retrospective outputs shall be shared with the Government and incorporated into the team's continuous improvement plan. Quarterly roadmaps reflecting planned feature delivery shall be maintained and updated following each sprint review.

The Government anticipates performance will be conducted at the contractor's facility. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Omaha District, is seeking proposals for facility maintenance and repair services at Camp Ashland, Nebraska. The source selection methodology for this procurement is Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA). Offerors must submit a Technical Volume and a Price Volume. The Technical Volume will be evaluated to determine whether the offeror's proposed approach meets the minimum requirements of the Performance Work Statement. Ratings will be limited to Acceptable or Unacceptable. An offeror receiving an Unacceptable technical rating will not be eligible for award. Price will be evaluated for completeness, reasonableness, and balance. Award will be made to the offeror with the lowest evaluated price whose technical proposal has been rated Acceptable. The use of LPTA in this acquisition is consistent with the determination that the requirement is well-defined and risk of unsuccessful performance is minimal. The Government does not anticipate the need for discussions.

Example 2:
The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Baltimore District, is soliciting proposals for environmental remediation and site restoration services at a formerly used defense site located in the Mid-Atlantic region. Given the scale of contamination, the complexity of multi-media remediation (soil, groundwater, and sediment), and the requirement for concurrent work across three distinct operable units, the Government has determined that a minimum of fifteen (15) qualified personnel must be engaged on this contract at all times during active remediation phases. The required team shall include a licensed Professional Engineer serving as Project Manager, a Certified Industrial Hygienist, at minimum two (2) licensed Geologists, a Health and Safety Officer, four (4) field technicians, a quality control manager, and sufficient support staff to maintain documentation, community relations, and regulatory liaison functions. The period of performance is estimated at thirty-six (36) months with two (2) twelve-month option periods. Offerors shall demonstrate in their Management Plan that the proposed team possesses the depth of expertise and numerical sufficiency to execute overlapping work streams without degradation of quality or schedule. Failure to staff to the required minimum levels at any point during performance may constitute a material breach of contract.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Region 5, is seeking a qualified contractor to conduct a Phase I Environmental Site Assessment and prepare a corresponding written report for a brownfield site located in metropolitan Chicago, Illinois. The firm-fixed-price contract resulting from this solicitation will carry a total obligated value of $47,500. This amount is inclusive of all labor, travel, laboratory analysis coordination, report preparation, and incidental expenses. No additional funding is available, and the Government will not negotiate modifications to increase the contract ceiling. The period of performance is ninety (90) calendar days from date of award. This procurement is reserved for businesses certified under the 8(a) Business Development Program. The independent government cost estimate supporting this solicitation was developed in accordance with EPA acquisition policy and confirms that $47,500 is commensurate with current market rates for equivalent assessment services. All costs associated with the delivery of final work products must fall within the stated contract amount.

SECTION C – DESCRIPTION/SPECIFICATIONS/STATEMENT OF WORK

C.1 Background. The General Services Administration's Public Buildings Service requires an independent structural assessment of a single historic federal courthouse building located in the Pacific Northwest region. The Department of Transportation Federal Highway Administration hereby requests proposals for an enterprise asset management platform supporting the National Bridge Inspection Program. Consistent with FHWA's technology modernization policy, this acquisition mandates a COTS solution and explicitly prohibits any custom application development. Vendors shall not include development labor categories, software engineering hours, or coding deliverables within their proposed staffing plans. The work under this contract is scoped as configuration only, covering asset taxonomy definition, inspection workflow setup, geospatial layer integration using the platform's native mapping connectors, and report template customization through the product's built-in report designer. The Contractor must demonstrate that all proposed capabilities are available in the current production release of the software and do not depend on future product updates or vendor commitments. Proposals referencing software development lifecycles, code repositories, or testing environments associated with new feature creation will be considered outside the scope of this solicitation and may result in the proposal being found technically unacceptable.

Example 2:
The General Services Administration Office of Technology Policy is issuing this Request for Proposal for a cloud-based contract writing and acquisition management platform. This procurement is strictly limited to the acquisition of an existing commercial solution. No software development is authorized under any circumstances. Offerors shall not propose to build, code, or engineer new application modules in response to agency-specific requirements. The Government's intent is to leverage a proven, market-available product and adapt internal business processes to align with the tool's existing capabilities. Configuration of user permissions, workflow routing, document templates, and integration with existing financial systems via standard APIs is permissible and expected. Offerors must demonstrate that the proposed platform has been deployed in at least three federal agency environments. Proposals that include statements of work referencing sprint cycles, agile development backlogs, or custom feature engineering will be evaluated unfavorably and may be deemed non-compliant with this solicitation's scope restrictions.

Example 3:
The Department of Veterans Affairs is seeking a Commercial Off-the-Shelf (COTS) solution for enterprise human resources management. Offerors must provide a fully licensed, commercially available software platform that meets the functional requirements outlined in this solicitation. No custom software development will be performed under this contract. The Government requires configuration only to align the solution with VA-specific workflows, user roles, and reporting hierarchies. Vendors who propose proprietary or purpose-built applications developed specifically for this requirement will be considered non-responsive. The selected solution must be a product currently available in the commercial marketplace with an established user base of no fewer than fifty enterprise customers. Implementation services shall be limited to system configuration, data migration, and user training. All modifications to core application functionality are expressly prohibited. The Contractor shall document all configuration settings to ensure repeatability and auditability. Any identified gaps between COTS functionality and agency requirements shall be addressed through process re-engineering on the Government side, not through development of new software features.

The contractor shall implement Agile development methodologies throughout the period of performance. All software development activities shall be conducted using Scrum framework, with two-week sprint cycles. The contractor shall conduct daily stand-up meetings, sprint planning sessions, sprint reviews, and sprint retrospectives in accordance with Scrum ceremonies. A certified Scrum Master shall be designated and responsible for facilitating all Agile ceremonies and removing impediments to team progress. The Government Product Owner shall participate in sprint planning and backlog grooming sessions to prioritize user stories and acceptance criteria. The contractor shall maintain a product backlog in a Government-approved project management tool and provide sprint velocity metrics as part of monthly progress reporting. All deliverables shall be produced incrementally, with a potentially shippable product increment demonstrated at the conclusion of each sprint. The contractor shall adhere to the Agile Manifesto principles and demonstrate continuous improvement through retrospective action items documented and tracked across sprints.

Example 2:
The General Services Administration Office of Information Technology requires offerors to demonstrate proven experience delivering projects using Agile development practices. The period of performance shall be executed through iterative Agile sprints of two-week duration. The contractor shall implement all five Scrum events: Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective, and the Sprint itself, with Government personnel invited and encouraged to participate in Sprint Reviews and Retrospectives. The contractor's Agile Coach shall work with the Government Product Owner to build and maintain a prioritized product backlog, ensuring that the highest-value features are delivered earliest. Acceptance testing shall be performed within the sprint in which development occurs, consistent with Agile testing best practices and shift-left quality principles. The contractor shall track and report on Agile health metrics including team happiness index, sprint goal achievement rate, and escaped defect rate. All tools used to manage the Agile process, including the backlog and sprint boards, shall be accessible to designated Government personnel at all times.

Example 3:
The Department of Transportation, Federal Aviation Administration, solicits technical and price proposals for development of the Aeronautical Information Management Replacement System. Given the safety-critical nature of the system, the FAA mandates compliance with DO-178C and requires the use of traditional SDLC sequential phases throughout the program lifecycle. The contractor shall complete and obtain Government approval of all systems engineering artifacts associated with each development phase before proceeding to subsequent phases. The development sequence shall include: System Requirements Review, Software Requirements Review, Preliminary Design Review, Critical Design Review, Software Build and Integration, Software Qualification Testing, System Acceptance Testing, and Operational Readiness Review. A fixed requirements baseline shall be established following Government approval of the System and Software Requirements Specifications. The contractor shall employ rigorous phase gate controls, and no phase transition shall occur without documented Government authorization. Traceability matrices shall be maintained from requirements through test.

This solicitation seeks a qualified research institution or academic consortium to conduct an independent longitudinal study examining how municipalities have historically evaluated enterprise software procurement decisions, including their documented experiences with off-the-shelf solutions, proprietary platforms, and hybrid development approaches. The research scope encompasses qualitative case analysis of procurement outcomes across no fewer than forty jurisdictions over a fifteen-year observation period. Offerors should note that this contract does not involve the purchase, licensing, configuration, or deployment of any software system whatsoever. The Government is procuring analytical research services only. The study shall examine, among other variables, the total cost of ownership associated with commercial platform adoption versus custom development, the frequency with which agencies that adopted pre-built platforms subsequently required significant re-engineering, and the governance factors that predicted successful technology procurement outcomes. Researchers shall apply mixed-methods approaches combining structured interviews with procurement officials, quantitative analysis of public contracting records, and review of post-implementation audit findings. Deliverables include quarterly progress reports, a comprehensive methodology document, peer-review-ready manuscripts, and a final policy brief formatted for consumption by senior agency leadership. The contractor shall coordinate with the agency's Chief Information Officer's staff to ensure findings are contextualized within current federal IT modernization guidance and are consistent with applicable OMB policies governing technology investment review.

The Scrum Master shall facilitate sprint retrospectives and shall maintain a retrospective action log accessible to the Government. Agile metrics shall be reported in the contractor's monthly status report, including planned versus completed story points, sprint goal achievement rate, and the number of impediments raised and resolved. The contractor shall provide Agile coaching support to Government personnel as a secondary deliverable to build internal agency capacity.

Example 3:
The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services requires the contractor to apply Agile methodology across all workstreams supporting the modernization of legacy claims processing systems. The contractor shall organize work into user stories, epics, and themes within a hierarchical backlog structure and shall demonstrate traceability from individual user stories to program-level epics and strategic objectives. Sprints shall be conducted on a two-week cadence with sprint planning occurring on the first day of each sprint and sprint review and retrospective conducted on the final day. The Government's designated Product Owner shall have authority to accept or reject sprint deliverables based on the agreed Definition of Done. The contractor shall not carry incomplete user stories across sprint boundaries without documented justification and re-estimation. The contractor shall facilitate quarterly Agile maturity assessments using a Government-approved framework and shall present findings and improvement recommendations to the CMS program office. Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Continuous Integration practices shall be employed as integral components of the Agile engineering workflow.

The National Institutes of Health, Office of Information Technology, intends to award a cost-plus-fixed-fee task order for the modernization of its grants management portal. All software design, development, testing, and deployment activities shall be performed in accordance with Agile/Scrum methodology as defined herein. The contractor shall establish cross-functional Scrum teams composed of a Scrum Master, Product Owner liaison, front-end and back-end developers, UX designers, and quality assurance engineers. Each sprint shall be time-boxed to two weeks, beginning with a sprint planning ceremony in which the team selects user stories from the prioritized product backlog and commits to a sprint goal. The contractor shall adhere to a mutually agreed-upon Definition of Done for all user stories, encompassing unit testing, integration testing, code review, and successful deployment to the staging environment. Agile metrics including sprint burndown, release burnup, and team velocity shall be maintained and made available to NIH leadership upon request.

The primary work under this contract is configuring and deploying the selected commercial off-the-shelf platform to meet DHS-specific reporting, resource allocation, and milestone tracking requirements. The contractor shall configure dashboards, automated notifications, role-based access controls, and integration connectors to existing financial management systems. Offerors shall describe their approach to Salesforce configuration without reliance on custom Apex code development except where configuration-only approaches are technically infeasible and documented as such. The Government anticipates a total level of effort of approximately 2,800 hours across the base year, with options for ongoing platform administration and enhancement.

NOTICE OF SET-ASIDE: The Federal Highway Administration (FHWA), in accordance with the Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program established under 15 U.S.C. 637(m) and implemented at FAR Subpart 19.15, hereby designates this procurement as a total set-aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business (EDWOSB) concerns. The FHWA requires professional environmental consulting services to support National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) compliance reviews for proposed highway infrastructure projects in the Mountain West region. The applicable NAICS code is 541620 – Environmental Consulting Services, with a size standard of $19 million in average annual receipts. The contracting officer's market research has determined that there is a reasonable expectation that two or more EDWOSB concerns capable of performing the required work at a fair market price will submit offers. Eligible offerors must be certified as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses through the SBA's EDWOSB certification process. Firms that are certified only as Women-Owned Small Businesses (WOSB) but not as Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses are not eligible for this set-aside. All EDWOSB certifications must be current and reflected in SAM.gov at time of offer.

The contractor shall implement Agile development methodologies throughout the period of performance. All software development activities shall be conducted using Scrum framework, with two-week sprint cycles. The contractor shall conduct daily stand-up meetings, sprint planning sessions, sprint reviews, and sprint retrospectives in accordance with Scrum ceremonies. A certified Scrum Master shall be designated and responsible for facilitating all Agile ceremonies and removing impediments to team progress. The Government Product Owner shall participate in sprint planning and backlog grooming sessions to prioritize user stories and acceptance criteria. The contractor shall maintain a product backlog in a Government-approved project management tool and provide sprint velocity metrics as part of monthly progress reporting. Contractors shall conduct sprint planning meetings with SSA's Product Owner to decompose epics into sprint-ready user stories with acceptance criteria no later than the iteration planning session preceding each sprint. The contractor shall update the Government's enterprise project management tool daily with sprint board status, impediments, and burndown data. The contractor shall implement Definition of Ready criteria to ensure user stories are sufficiently detailed prior to sprint commitment. Quarterly business reviews shall include Agile performance metrics, including average sprint velocity, defect density, and sprint goal completion percentage.

Velocity data, sprint goal achievement rates, and escaped defect counts shall be reported in the bi-weekly Integrated Program Management Report submitted to the NASA Contracting Officer's Representative.

Transition activities shall include a mandatory 90-day knowledge transfer period during which the outgoing and incoming contractors will operate concurrently. The Government requires that the new contractor review all existing technical documentation, data dictionaries, and system design records prior to assuming full operational responsibility. Offerors must describe in their technical approach how they will manage risk during transition from incumbent operations, including how they will handle undocumented components of the existing system. Continuity of service to Veterans during the transition period is a non-negotiable requirement, and the Government will evaluate all proposals on the basis of transition risk mitigation strategies.

Example 3:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Information and Technology, hereby solicits proposals for the development and deployment of a patient scheduling optimization tool for use at one regional VA medical center. The total ceiling for this contract shall not exceed $85,000 for a base period of performance of twelve (12) months. No option periods are anticipated at this time. Offerors are advised that all technical deliverables, including requirements documentation, system design, development, testing, training, and post-deployment support, must be accomplished within the stated ceiling amount. The Government does not intend to award a contract exceeding this threshold under any circumstances. This acquisition is set aside exclusively for small businesses under NAICS code 541511. Interested parties should note that the contracting officer has determined that $85,000 represents a fair and reasonable price for the described scope of work based on independent government cost estimates. Proposals that exceed this maximum budget will be deemed non-responsive and will not be evaluated further. Offerors must clearly demonstrate how all required tasks can be completed within the $85,000 total contract value.

Example 1:
The Social Security Administration, Office of Systems, issues this Request for Proposal for the Disability Determination Services Workflow Automation Project. The contractor shall manage all development activities in accordance with SSA's Enterprise Systems Development Methodology, which prescribes a waterfall approach to system delivery. Project execution shall follow sequential phases as follows: Business Requirements Definition, Logical Design, Physical Design, Construction, System Integration Testing, User Acceptance Testing, and Production Deployment. Each phase produces mandatory deliverables that must receive formal written acceptance from the SSA Project Manager before work on the subsequent phase is authorized to begin. The contractor shall establish and maintain a fixed requirements baseline upon Government acceptance of the Business Requirements Specification. All proposed modifications to baselined requirements shall be submitted via the SSA Change Request process and are subject to impact assessment, Configuration Control Board review, and written approval prior to incorporation.

Example 2:
The General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, hereby solicits offers for development of the Facilities Asset Lifecycle Tracking System. In accordance with GSA Order CIO 2100.1, the contractor shall utilize standard software lifecycle development practices as the governing development framework for this engagement. The project shall be organized into five sequential phases: Requirements Gathering, Architecture and Design, Development, Testing and Quality Assurance, and Deployment and Transition. Phase-to-phase progression is contingent upon successful completion of formal Government review and written authorization to proceed. The contractor is expressly prohibited from initiating coding activities prior to approval of the Functional Requirements Document and System Architecture Document. A fixed requirements baseline shall be established no later than 60 calendar days after contract award. Subsequent changes to baselined requirements must be processed through the formal Engineering Change Proposal process and approved in writing by the Contracting Officer.

Example 3:
The Department of Justice, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, solicits proposals for the Federal Firearms Licensee Inspection Management System. The contractor shall develop the system in accordance with ATF's Information Systems Security and Development Policy, which requires use of a standard software lifecycle development approach for all major applications. The development effort shall be organized into the following sequential phases: Initiation and Planning, Requirements Analysis, System Design, Development and Unit Testing, Integration Testing, User Acceptance Testing, Security Assessment and Authorization, and Production Deployment. Each phase shall conclude with a formal milestone review and production of Government-accepted phase deliverables before the subsequent phase is initiated. A fixed requirements baseline shall be documented and approved in writing no later than 45 days following contract award. The Department of Agriculture, Forest Service, issues this solicitation for tree marking, timber cruising, and silvicultural services in the Tongass National Forest, Alaska. Source selection will be accomplished using the lowest price technically acceptable methodology. The Technical Factor will assess whether the offeror has demonstrated the minimum qualifications, past relevant experience, and equipment necessary to perform the work as described in the Statement of Work. Technical acceptability determinations will be made on a pass/fail basis only. Price will be evaluated as the total price for all contract line items over the ordering period. Among all offerors determined to be technically acceptable, the award will be issued to the offeror presenting the lowest price. The Government has determined that LPTA is appropriate for this acquisition because the work is routine in nature, performance requirements are clearly defined, and successful performance risk is low. Offerors are cautioned that elaborate technical proposals will not improve their competitive standing.

Example 2:
The Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Acquisition Solutions, is issuing this Request for Proposals for technical support services related to water quality monitoring and sampling at Superfund sites in EPA Region 5. This acquisition will be evaluated using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process in accordance with FAR 15.101-2. Technical acceptability will be evaluated under three pass/fail criteria: Key Personnel Qualifications, Laboratory Accreditation Compliance, and Quality Assurance Plan Adequacy. Proposals must be rated Acceptable on all three criteria to be eligible for award. Technical proposals that exceed minimum requirements will receive no additional consideration. Following determination of technical acceptability, award will be made to the responsible offeror submitting the lowest evaluated total price. The Government has made a written determination that LPTA is the appropriate evaluation approach given the well-defined nature of the work and the availability of qualified contractors capable of performing at minimum standards. Questions regarding the LPTA evaluation criteria must be submitted by the deadline specified in Section L.

Example 3:
This solicitation is issued by the General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service, for grounds maintenance and landscaping services at federal facilities in the National Capital Region. Source selection will be conducted using the Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) methodology as prescribed in FAR 15.101-2. Evaluation factors include Technical Capability and Price. Technical Capability will be rated either Acceptable or Unacceptable based on the offeror's demonstrated ability to meet the requirements of the Statement of Work. Price will not be evaluated for technical merit. Award will be made to the offeror submitting the lowest price among all technically acceptable proposals. Proposals rated Unacceptable under the Technical Capability factor will be eliminated from consideration regardless of price. The Government reserves the right to conduct discussions if necessary but intends to make award without discussions. All offerors must be registered in SAM.gov at time of offer submission.

Example 1:
Section M – Evaluation Criteria: The Department of Homeland Security will evaluate all responsive proposals using a Best Value Tradeoff methodology. Following written proposal evaluation, offerors deemed within the competitive range will be invited to deliver an oral presentation to the Source Selection Evaluation Board (SSEB). The oral presentation is intended to allow offerors to clarify and elaborate upon their written technical submissions and to demonstrate the depth of understanding of the program requirements. Presentations shall be limited to sixty (60) minutes with an additional twenty (20) minutes reserved for Government questions. The lead proposed Program Manager and at least two key technical personnel identified in the proposal must participate in person. Remote participation via video teleconference will be permitted only upon written approval by the Contracting Officer. Scores assigned during the oral presentation phase will be integrated with written proposal scores to produce a final technical rating for each offeror.

Example 2:
Offerors in the competitive range shall be required to participate in an oral presentation as part of the evaluation process. The oral presentation will be conducted at the Department of Veterans Affairs Network Contracting Office and will be scheduled at the Government's discretion. The purpose of the oral presentation is to assess each offeror's understanding of the Performance Work Statement requirements, proposed staffing model, and quality control methodology. Presentations shall be a maximum of seventy-five (75) minutes in duration, which includes a mandatory fifteen (15) minute question-and-answer session conducted by the Technical Evaluation Panel. The proposed Contract Program Manager must attend and lead the presentation. Supporting staff may participate but shall not exceed four (4) individuals total. All slide materials or visual aids must be provided to the Contracting Officer no later than three (3) business days prior to the presentation. The Government may record oral presentations for evaluation documentation purposes, and offerors will be notified accordingly.

Example 3:
As part of the evaluation methodology for this Information Technology Services procurement, the General Services Administration (GSA) will incorporate oral presentations into the source selection process. After an initial review of written proposals, offerors receiving a rating of Acceptable or higher under the Technical Factor will be invited to participate in an oral presentation before the Technical Evaluation Team. The oral presentation allows the Government to assess offeror capabilities beyond what is conveyed in written form and to evaluate proposed personnel directly. Each offeror will be allotted ninety (90) minutes total: sixty (60) minutes for the presentation and thirty (30) minutes for Government-directed questions. Key personnel named in Section H must be present and actively participate. Substitutions of key personnel for the oral presentation will not be permitted without prior written approval and may negatively impact the evaluation. Offerors should note that the oral presentation is evaluated separately under Factor 2 – Management Approach and scored on an Adjectival basis consistent with the criteria described in Section M.

3.4 Source Selection Procedures. The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) intends to award a single Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract resulting from this solicitation using a Best Value Tradeoff source selection approach. Written technical proposals will be evaluated first by the Source Selection Evaluation Board (SSEB). Following the completion of initial written evaluations, offerors whose proposals are assessed as technically acceptable and placed within the competitive range will be scheduled to deliver an oral presentation before the SSEB and subject matter experts designated by the Contracting Officer. USACE will provide written notification of scheduled oral presentation dates no fewer than seven (7) calendar days prior to the scheduled session. Offerors may not substitute personnel who participate in the oral presentation for different individuals in the resulting contract without prior written approval from the Contracting Officer. Any inconsistencies identified between statements made during the oral presentation and the written proposal will be addressed through the clarification or discussion process prior to final evaluation.
